likes: I find Xero easy to use and customizable, making it a one-stop shop for my needs. I appreciate that it keeps everything in one place, serving as a single source of truth that captures income and expenditure correctly. It's capable of producing essential reports and handling returns like BAS, Tax, EOFY along with various health reports for multiple companies and currencies. I love that it captures data in real time and allows multiple users to access it from anywhere in the world. The payroll features are quick and easy to use, it links to the ATO and Super payments, and is compliant and secure. I enjoy how Xero integrates well with other systems and can export and manipulate data effectively. It's a great accounting and payroll package.
dislikes: There are a few clunky bits, especially with payroll and how it calculates wages, plus some reports/analysis we used to have in EH we don't have in Xero. Also, it's data/transaction limited and we reached the quota, so to speak, and the whole system slowed down.
use_case: Xero keeps everything in one place, serving as a single source of truth for income, expenditure, and reporting. It's quick and secure, allowing multiple users access anywhere globally. It integrates with other systems, simplifies forecasting, and makes payroll management quick and easy. Effortless Accounting, Minor

likes: I use Xero for my small business accounting and it's the only software I have, which does the job well without any functionality complaints. I find it easy to use even as an amateur, which is important since I don't hire an accountant and handle everything myself. Xero's features are good at showing the financial situation and entering records, and I particularly like the reconciliation UI. The initial setup was easy, and although I had to learn some accounting concepts, Xero helped me understand better through real examples. I also appreciate that it connects with my payroll service, Wagepoint. The performance is reliable. I think the pricing is reasonable. I think the AI integration is limited at this point.
dislikes: bank account sync doesn't include Wealthsimple
use_case: I use Xero for my small business accounting. It's the only software I have, and it works well without complaints. It's easy to use, even for an amateur like me. I like Xero's reconciliation UI and its ability to show the situation through entry of records. Perfect for Solo

likes: The most important thing for me is ease of use, especially when it comes to the app and desktop interface. Xero has a format that’s very pleasant to navigate, with good colour contrast.
dislikes: Moderately confusing is the ability to refer a friend for commission rewards. I opted in to this, yet it isn't a fixed to my Xero account, thus I had to create two separate accounts. Moreover, the scheme seems to be available through external providers such as 'Partnerstack' too, which complicates the service further.
use_case: Accountancy tools should be simple to use. The drive should be to make a likely mundane task for most seem less daunting. I don't think a multitude of features drives the market. One could use fintech invest apps for comparison. The target audience is the average member of the public which is where sps like QuickBooks which I used briefly some tears ago, fail. Their interface is too much geared towards the professional sphere which is off putting for consumers such as myself. If it seems too complex I'm not incentivised to undertake tasks and will look elsewhere for an alternative, hence Xero. Pleasant, Easy-to-Navigate Interface with Great

likes: It makes reconciling easy and makes for beautifully professional invoices. It does separate from the bank feed regularly, and no one seems to know how to fix this. Support is generally pretty helpful besides this issue. The AI bot hasn't been that helpful as it keeps repeating the same answers. The pricing is competitive but it used to be really affordable. This made it easier to take when the feed brakes and the glitches come up from our tax code not being in the software.
dislikes: I included all in the last part but the bank feed regularly separates and no one knows how to fix this. I have to keep playing with it until it rejoins. Also, there are a few glitches that come from our tax information not being in the system, such as when I refunded a customer's delivery fee and it is still trying to refund tax associated with the fee, when there is none.
use_case: I am not a heavy user of Xero. We just use it for simple reconciliation, invoices here and there and checking P&L, comparing months, etc. We don't use it for any complex purposes. Maybe tiers for people like me might be a good option? Great Invoicing and Reconciliation, but Bank Feed Disconnects and Tax

likes: I use Xero for accounting in my business, and it really helps me with invoicing, collecting payments, tracking costs, running payroll, and submitting my VAT and Annual returns. It just works and is very simple, helping me get things right. It's also widely used, so others know how it works. This is hugely valuable to me because mistakes can cost a lot in fines and scrutiny, and as a non-accountant, it's great to learn from it. The initial setup was very simple, and I loved that part. It was very quick to get started. The platform integrates with a large number of providers which is great and its well priced for a small business, considering how much you get and there is a lot included in the base packages. Ai support is also useful and overall performs well when we need it.
dislikes: Sometimes when I want to edit something from the past, it can be a little annoying. Also, payments against expenses could be smoother as a process. I think this could be handled in a single step, rather than having to recognize the expense, then make the payment - it just feels like there's a lot going on. It could be really simple.
use_case: Xero enables me to invoice, collect payments, keep track of costs, run payroll, and submit VAT and Annual returns. It's simple to use and helps avoid costly mistakes. It's so good to be able to feel confident in our numbers and know we have plenty of detail if we are ever asked. Effortless Accounting, Perfect for

likes: I find Xero to be an incredibly easy choice for managing my consulting business, thanks to its ease of use and effective yet lightweight functionality. The pricing, especially with their recent promo, made it much more affordable than alternatives like QuickBooks. I love how it simplifies reconciling transactions by allowing me to easily connect my business bank account and reconcile weekly. The user-friendly interface makes designating expenses as billable super easy, which is invaluable as a consultant. The recently overhauled dashboard provides a great snapshot of outstanding items and financial performance, which I really enjoy. Not having to pay for an extra seat to share data directly with my CPA is super helpful. I also appreciate that onboarding was guided with helpful emails and resources, even though I didn't need them due to my prior familiarity with Xero.
dislikes: I wish that Xero had more beautiful charts. I love a Sankey diagram, and it would be nice to see the cash flow in and cash flow out in an elegant chart. While it's pretty easy to label expenses or transactions as a billable expense, it takes several extra clicks that I wish it didn't require. Sometimes it's just not intuitive to me, maybe because I'm not an accountant or daily user of the software.
use_case: I use Xero for my single shingle consulting business. It simplifies reconciling transactions and makes billing clients a breeze inlcuding incurred costs. The recently overhauled dashboard provides a financial snapshot at a glance, and I can invite my CPA without extra charges, saving time and effort. Efficient and User-Friendly, Perfect for Small

likes: I really like Xero's clean look and simplified navigation. It's great being able to drill down into almost any number and quickly find out what's behind it. This is really handy for accessing information fast when working across multiple clients. It's also very intuitive and seamless, making it easy to understand and assuring me that it's robust. I feel confident that when I see a number on a balance sheet, I can quickly see all the transactions behind it. Additionally, Xero's modern and cloud-based structure makes it easy to correct mistakes and understand numbers, which is a huge advantage over our previous software. The initial setup was incredibly easy, which was a pleasant surprise.
dislikes: I guess group companies could be an area for improvement. At the moment if you manage say have businesses within a group, it would be great to be able to link group companies, helping record the transfer of cash and live managing of balances. Xero seems to work very well with a single entity business unit. But, there's obviously no way to link group or consolidation transactions.
use_case: I use Xero to understand our business's financial position, manage books, and produce monthly accounts. It helps ensure costs and revenue are captured well and provides quick access to detailed transactional information. Effortless Accounting with

switched_from.reason: We needed a much faster and simpler finance system for the business

likes:
Xero makes accounting tasks easy, whether I’m setting up a new client, managing the day-to-day work, or integrating with other tools. It’s stable and reliable, and it offers a lot of additional features for a reasonable monthly price. I also love how streamlined the interface is: there are no pop-ups, ads, or other distractions, so I can stay focused on getting work done. Support from Xero is quick, and they go the extra mile to help resolve any issues that come up. I’m also really looking forward to the expansion with Claude for business insights.
The different buckets for invoices - draft, submitted and approved - are such a useful tool for managing a client's invoices so that future revenue is has visibility and nothing gets forgotten.
The two tracking categories also provide great dimensional reporting.
dislikes: I'd love to see better project based reporting, but there are a lot of other apps that integrate so well with Xero, that it hasn't been a big limiting factor. I would also love it if we could choose to not see archived accounts when creating new reports, as this creates a bit of extra work in combing through them. More dimensional reporting with additional tracking categories would also be helpful. Lastly, and I think my biggest ask, is the ability to reconcile ALL balance sheet accounts - this is one thing that QBO has that I'd love to see in Xero.
use_case: The new auto reconcile feature is a huge improvement right now. It is causing me to look at the types of employees that we as an accounting firm want to hire, since data entry isn't really a big lift any longer. This makes us want to hire more experienced bookkeepers, and not have to pay them to do a lot of basic data entry work. This allows us to focus more on a client's reporting and analysis work, rather than just getting the monthly reports out the door. That helps our clients make better business decisions even faster. Xero Makes Accounting Effortless with a Clean Interface and Reliable

switched_from.reason: I switched because we needed to move all of our clients to the cloud (back in 2013), as I was moving to a new state. This allowed me to bring all my clients with me and keep my business alive.

likes: What I like most about Xero is that it's not Intuit. QBO is junk. Xero has its quirks, but overall it's very easy to get stuff done once you take the time to understand how Xero does things versus QBO. I especially like the sability of the bank feeds and the ease of reconciliation.
dislikes: I don't really love their integration with Melio, athough it seems to be getting better. I also think their interfaces are a bit dated.
use_case: The primary problem is that my data now exists in the cloud and as a result I can quickly jump in each day and reconcile transactions on the fly. This is a huge time saver. Additionally there are just a lot fewer bookkeeping steps when using Xero versus QBD. Evenutually I hope to hook this all into an AI workflow to really save time. Stable Bank Feeds and Easy Reconciliation Make

switched_from.reason: Intuit is garbage.

likes: The thing that I like most about Xero is its smooth processing of real-time financial visibility in regular business activities. When working with real-life situations, such as managing several clients or dealing with expenditures within projects, it becomes significantly easier to have a comprehensive and up-to-date understanding of cash flow, invoicing, and reconciliation processes. For instance, when analyzing financial statements at the end of each month, the system provides convenient access to the necessary information without requiring manual data aggregation. Moreover, it boasts an easy-to-use interface that even non-financial employees can operate using basic functions such as invoicing or spending documentation.
dislikes:
The first limitation I have observed while using Xero is that it has certain limitations regarding advanced reporting and customization options. For instance, while trying to create a report or view finances from different perspectives, one finds limited possibilities in doing so within Xero, hence having to resort to exporting the information and then manually manipulating it using other software programs.

Another drawback I have experienced when working with Xero is that when reconciling transactions or dealing with several of them at once, the procedure may turn out to be rather manual and, therefore, tedious. In addition, while many third-party applications integrate well with Xero, resulting in discrepancies in information that will require manual adjustments.

Finally, it should be noted that while Xero provides excellent accounting services, its pricing increases when using additional functions or involving more users, making the solution costly for large companies.
use_case:
One of the major benefits of Xero is its efficient approach to eliminating fragmentation and reducing manual labor in the management of finances, especially in environments that have traditionally operated using spreadsheet-based or fragmented software systems. The use of different applications for managing invoices, expense management, bank account reconciliations, and other financial-related tasks in practical settings is prone to causing unnecessary delays, discrepancies, and a greater probability of errors. This is where Xero comes in by integrating all these processes into one cohesive solution and streamlining most of them.

For instance, during day-to-day activities, users no longer need to spend time and effort reconciling accounts manually or tracking expenditures. Instead, the system can automatically pull in bank feed information and suggest reconciliations to help reduce manual labor. In situations involving the management of client billing, the preparation and sending of invoices are made faster and more efficient through the integration of payments and payment statuses.

In cases where financial information must be tracked in real-time such as for cash flow analysis or managing outstanding bills users are spared from having to compile data from various sources and then analyzing them. Great Real-Time Visibility and Ease of Use, but Reporting and Reconciliation

likes: I appreciate Xero for having a clean interface and being easy to use for both my team and the clients, despite the word choices taking some getting used to. The platform is stable, and the support is responsive and knowledgeable about the product. I find the cost to value very honest, making it a very economical choice for my clients. I also like the bulk categorizing of transactions as it's a good feature. The integration and conversion team seems solid, which has been helpful during setup.
dislikes: Making changes to transactions is a bit harder and not as intuitive. There is a learning curve with the language as well. There are some features that are very different than other systems. I was surprised to learn that you can move items around on your reports. It was not something I was expecting and the learning curve has been a interesting process.
use_case: I switched to Xero because it's economical and avoids high costs, AI enforcement, and interface changes. The clean interface and stable platform make it easy for my team and clients. The bulk categorization feature is particularly valuable. The main problem that is being solved is my client's desire to have a good alternative to other options. Most of the software systems are focusing on getting AI so fully involved that they are losing site of the fact that the end user might not want it. The ability to decide how much help they want. The user experience is more intuitive for the lay user as well which can make my life as a a teacher and a reviewer of the books much easier Cost-Effective, Stable Platform with

switched_from.reason: A number of my clients were unhappy with the daily changes in QBO, they did not want the forced AI experience and the cost was getting to be prohibitive

likes:
We’ve used Xero for a number of clients, mainly UK-based, and the interface is genuinely quite clean compared with some other tools. It’s straight to navigate, even if you’re not in it every day.

Bank feeds and reconciliation run pretty smoothly most of the time, and the match suggestions are actually useful for speeding things up during month end close.
dislikes: The search feature isn’t very good compared to QBO. The Manual Journal interface isn’t great either, and recurring journals can be a bit tricky to set up and manage.
use_case:
One thing that really good about is the integrations, especially with Gusto, Brex, and Mercury. The bill syncing from these platforms works really well and honestly saves a lot of time on the bookkeeping side. It reduces a lot of the manual work we’d otherwise have to do.

For us, it mainly helps in keeping client books more organized without spending too much time on initial setup. Reconciliation and day-to-day bookkeeping feel more streamlined, so we can spend more time actually reviewing things instead of just entering data. Monthly Accounting and

likes: The UI is very easy to use, and the software lets you seamlessly flick through different SPVs/companies so you can batch-approve payments. It also allows you to connect to your bank, which is a big time-saver. On top of that, you can use an API to improve efficiency, especially when it comes to reporting.
dislikes: There is no option to view all outstanding payments from all companies on Xero, instead you have to cycle through them one by one to manually check.
use_case: It saves a lot of time when completing payments, because you can request that they’re paid in one go. It then sends an automatic email to the accounts team, so they can set up the payments all at once. It also makes it easy for us to check P&L reports and quickly see how costs compare with budget figures. Easy-to-Use UI with Seamless SPV Switching, Bank Connections, and

likes: I liked how Xero branded itself as a SaaS accounting software, and integrations it provided.
dislikes:
Its an awful product. I can tell you the code architecture is not good, and that is why I think they have lot of bugs in system. The system fails even when I get a PayPal transaction. They charge you for features which are basic, but the fact is their basic features are broken. Their support is just to give you therapy hey do not be upset. 

The UI, yea its beautiful, but the reason is its a SaaS and based on probably .net framework that has itself very beautiful views and widgets. It is not something they have invented something in Xero the way they boast "Beautiful Business".

likes: I find Xero to be very streamlined and easy to use. It's an easy-to-operate platform, which makes it simple to navigate and locate what I need, like payslips and time management details. The user-friendly nature of Xero helps me log in without hassle, and the tabs are straightforward, allowing smooth navigation to view documents. The setup was also very easy and didn't take much time, which was convenient.
dislikes: I believe it can be more streamlined so that I'm not asked for zero verify every time; just a login would be great. Also, I think it should provide more information on how my fees are calculated, as sometimes it's just one document when it could be more informative.
use_case: I use Xero for viewing payslips and managing time; it's easy to navigate and helps me see crucial salary details. User-Friendly Accounting Platform with

likes: I like that it’s so user-friendly once you get the hang of it, and that it helps non-accounting-savvy people (like me) easily keep track of their books and finances. Especially for a sole proprietor like myself, it makes it so easy to implement and integrate into my weekly and daily business routines. Each week, 1-2 times per week, I log into Xero, reconcile all the transactions in my accounts and cards, and can rest easy knowing that my records are done, clean, and accurate!
dislikes: The only downsides about Xero is that the the user interface is a little clunky, not as sleek. There also could be more tutorials or "help" features to make onboarding and getting used to the program easier. Though of course I haven't consulted much of the customer support, so I'm sure they would be more than happy to help me walk through any issues or questions I have!
use_case: Xero takes so much of the stress of bookkeeping and accounting for sole proprietors away! Before Xero, I was up to my neck in spreadsheets and computer folders keeping track of my income, who I was paid by, what expenses I had made, etc. Now it's all automatically accumulated in my Xero dashboard, where I know that - with the press of a few "reconcile" buttons, I can easily keep my records clean. I cannot wait for tax time next year, when I'll just be able to run a few reports and input data, rather than spending hours calculating expenses and write-offs! User-Friendly Accounting for

likes: I love how Xero makes bookkeeping so much more streamlined and helps with invoicing and paying bills efficiently. As the business owner, it gives me a really good overview of how my business is doing. The reports, especially the profit and loss and the balance sheet, are something I love because they really help me keep my finger on the pulse. They're really useful, especially for digging deeper into our financials at the end of the month to analyze how we've done against our budget.
dislikes: The AI bill automation hasn't worked for me yet. It just didn't work, and it seems like quite a simple feature when I've uploaded a PDF. So it was quite disappointing.
use_case: Xero makes bookkeeping, invoicing, and paying bills so much more streamlined and gives me a really good overview of how the business is going. I love the reports, the profit and loss, and balance sheet, and I really feel like I've got my finger on the pulse. Streamlined Bookkeeping with Room for AI

likes: The program is quite robust, especially compared to the previous accounting software I was using. My accountant introduced it to me, and I can certainly see why they love it as much as they do. I typically log in 1-2 times a month to review my spending. I love how easy it is to track my receipts, spending, etc. Now that I reconcile my own books each month, the Hubdoc feature is especially helpful for matching my receipts to my spending. I also like how easy it is to integrate my credit cards so I don't have to worry about importing those transactions. I use a small, local credit union for banking, and though I do have to manually import transactions from those accounts via CSV files, it's VERY easy to do so and doesn't take much time at all. Xero's customer support is great and has been helpful when needed. Overall it's been a great tool on the accounting side of my small business.
dislikes: Though I've certainly become more familiar with the program, I don't feel that it's intuitive, especially if you're not familiar with reconciling transations.
use_case: Xero helps keep my business spending organized from month to month. I like that I can easily compare monthly reports. It helps to keep me ready come tax time. Robust Accounting Software with Effortless Receipt Tracking and Credit Card

switched_from.reason: I switched when I started working with my current accountant, as Xero is what they recommended. Xero is much more robust and offers countless more features than Quickbooks.

use_case:
Xero solves key accounting and bookkeeping challenges by centralizing financial data into one easy‑to‑use platform. It simplifies tasks like invoicing, bank reconciliations, expense tracking, and financial reporting while providing real‑time visibility into accounts.
This benefits us by saving time, reducing manual errors, improving accuracy, and enabling better financial control and decision‑making. The cloud‑based access also supports collaboration across teams and allows work to continue seamlessly from anywhere.

likes: It has a good easy to use interface and all the functions are easy to find. It has a good help system to get you through using those features that are not so obvious. The import bills functions by email is really useful for me and saves a lot of time over manually entering data.
dislikes: I have to spend a bit of time keeping the customer and supplier lists up to date. The merge feature that you use once this has happened to consolidate the records isn't the most intuitive and you can easily merge the records the wrong way.

With 24/7 access, strong inventory management capabilities, and integration with various apps, Xero provides a secure and scalable solution that helps streamline operations, improve cash flow visibility, and minimize manual data entry errors.

When payments come in, I reconcile them against invoices and keep everything organized in one place. I also track my software subscriptions and run basic reports to see where I stand each month.

likes:
I’m a freelance full-stack developer and used to track everything in spreadsheets. After about a year with Xero, I can’t imagine going back. I use it mainly for creating and sending invoices, reconciling bank transactions, and tracking expenses.
I’ve connected Stripe and PayPal so clients can pay directly from the invoice, which has definitely sped up payments. The bank feed pulls in transactions daily, and I usually spend a few minutes matching payments and categorizing expenses like software subscriptions or hosting. It’s way more organized than my old manual setup. I also check the Profit & Loss report every month to see how things are trending.
dislikes: Some of the accounting terms still feel a bit heavy. Setting things up the way my accountant wanted took some trial and error. Occasionally, the reconciliation suggestions aren’t perfect, especially with partial payments, so I have to double-check.
use_case: It keeps my finances in one place and makes year-end prep much easier. I can clearly see who’s paid, what’s outstanding, and how much I’ve actually made. For a solo freelancer, it fits well without feeling overcomplicated.

bonus_data.title: Made bookkeeping manageable for my freelance work

switched_from.reason: It saves lot of time and make workflow smooth I just use one tool.

likes: Xero is easier for people without bookkeeping experience to understand and use than other solutions while still adhering to accounting standards. They also offer free training to make it easier to use more effectively as your understanding grows.
dislikes: When using Xero with multiple clients, it's sometimes difficult to find where to access clients that you haven't touched in a while. This is a very minor issue and one that won't affect users that are using Xero for their own business.

We strive to educate our clients so they can use accounting as a management tool rather than just to help figure out their taxes. Xero's interface is logical and easy to navigate so they can explore and learn how to use it to find answers to their questions with only limited prompts from our bookkeepers and accountants.

bonus_data.title: Xero - powerful and scalable, easy to understand and use

likes: Xero’s greatest strength lies in its ability to humanize complex accounting through a world-class UI and its groundbreaking AI assistant, JAX, which allows users to manage finances via simple conversation rather than manual data entry. By combining a "beautiful" interface with an expansive ecosystem of 1,000+ integrations, it transforms from a simple ledger into a high-performance central nervous system that offers unlimited user collaboration and predictive 180-day cash flow forecasting. This blend of approachable design and sophisticated automated reconciliation—now boasting nearly 100% accuracy—ensures that business owners spend less time "doing the books" and more time acting on the high-level insights that drive ROI.
dislikes: Xero is perfect until you have a complex problem; then, the lack of a phone number to call and the rigid reporting make you feel like you've outgrown the 'beautiful' simplicity.
use_case: Ultimately, Xero is built to give you back time and mental bandwidth. It helps remove the “emotional tax” of business ownership—the stress of not knowing whether you can afford next month’s bills—by replacing guesswork with real-time, AI-powered insights. Beautiful UI Make

likes: I like Xero for its ability to handle all the bookkeeping issues that can arise when doing it manually. I find the automated rules incredibly helpful, along with accounting classification after reconciliation. Xero enables automated reconciliation from our ecommerce platform like Shopify, and we can keep up with expenses and client invoices seamlessly.
dislikes: The sales part of Xero could be improved, especially with the capacity to invoice partial quotes, like a deposit on an accepted quote. For example, I sell a product that needs 8-10 months for fabrication, and I should be able to invoice 50 or 60% of the quote and the balance when the product is ready to ship. Also, during the initial setup, Xero announced a free migration, which was not true. We had to pay $300 as we had more than one year of data. Additionally, the migration did not bring 100% of the database, as no estimate was imported.
use_case: Xero solves bookkeeping issues of manual accounting and provides automated reconciliation from our ecommerce platform, helping us keep up with expenses or client invoices efficiently. Streamlined Automation, Needs Sales Feature

likes: I find Xero incredibly easy to manage, especially for reconciling my bank accounts. It allows me to match and categorize my outgoings without hassle. I also really like the mileage recording activity, which is quite handy. Uploading receipts directly into the app by taking a photo from my phone makes it super easy to get things in the system while I'm on the go. The initial setup of Xero was incredibly easy, very simple to just set up and go.
dislikes: I was a bit confused by the expenses if I'm creating an expense for a contractor, for example, or to make a payment to a different business that I was in initiating. I tried to add the contractor, and then, it wouldn't let me save because the contractor hadn't previously been entered in the system. And then I have to authorize, but it wouldn't let me authorize that. And I seem to end up going back and forth between screens just to get this person in the system. And all I wanted to do was list it as an expense so that I could pay it.
use_case: Xero makes it easy to manage bank account reconciliation by matching and categorizing expenses. I like being able to upload receipts via photo, which ensures they don't get lost or forgotten. Effortless Receipt Management, Easy

likes: I liked how simple and clean the dashboard felt, making it easy to navigate between invoices, reports, and expense tracking. I also appreciated the cloud-based access because it made the platform easy to use from different devices. The initial setup was fairly simple, with the dashboard onboarding steps being easy to follow, allowing me to explore the main features without much difficulty. My experience during the trial was positive overall. The platform was easy to use and good for managing invoices and accounting tasks.
dislikes: Some advanced settings and reporting sections were a little confusing at first and took time to understand. I also noticed that new useful features seem to be available only in higher plans.
use_case: Xero organized my invoices, expenses, and financial information all in one place, making tracking and reviewing accounting data much easier. I also enjoyed cloud-based access for easy use across different devices. Easy Accounting and

likes:
The most impressive part of Xero’s recent evolution is how it has shifted from a reactive ledger to a proactive management tool. The integration of JAX (Just Ask Xero) is not just a gimmick; it has significantly reduced the time I spend running manual reports. Being able to ask for a "quick breakdown of revenue by service line" and getting a formatted visual in seconds is a massive productivity win.
Additionally, the automated bank reconciliation continues to be the gold standard. In 2026, the AI's ability to recognize intent—predicting account codes for complex transactions based on historical patterns rather than just simple rules—has cut our month-end closing time by nearly 30%. It feels less like data entry and more like data verification.
dislikes: While the core platform is excellent, the tier-based feature gating can still be frustrating. For example, needing a higher-tier plan or a third-party app just for advanced inventory or multi-warehouse tracking feels like a bit of a leap for mid-sized businesses. I also think the user interface consistency needs work. While the new dashboard and reporting sections are sleek and modern, navigating back into the deeper settings or the Chart of Accounts feels like stepping back five years. A unified design language across the entire platform would make the user experience much smoother.
use_case: The primary problem Xero solves for us is the "Financial Information Gap." Before Xero, we were always looking at the previous month's data to make today's decisions. Now, with real-time bank feeds and the Single Ledger approach, my accountant and I are always aligned. This transparency allows us to be far more agile. We can spot cash flow dips before they happen and pivot our spending without the anxiety of "not knowing the numbers." It has essentially democratized our financial data, making it useful for the whole leadership team, not just the finance department. A Scalable Backbone for Our

likes: I use Xero as the accounting software for my S Corp because it makes it easy for me to track financial transactions in a true accounting package. The initial setup was relatively easy, and I don't remember any problems.
dislikes: Sometimes I get purchase orders from clients and would like a way to upload them and then create my invoices against client POs. This may be a feature of a higher-level plan that I'm not currently subscribed to. But it's not a critical feature for me.

On the other hand, Xero's pricing plan is flexible which makes it accessible for many. Its customer service is convenient, and I would prefer a real time agent would talk it through. likes: Xero’s biggest strength is how intuitive and clean the platform is. The interface is easy to navigate, making daily bookkeeping and month-end work straightforward. What I like best—something I haven’t found on other platforms—is the ability to fully customize account transaction reports. You can select exactly which columns to display, filter, toggle options on and off, and group or summarize transactions by any category you need. This makes it easy to generate reports that actually answer specific questions, saving time and making book review much more efficient.

dislikes: There are 2 small downsides to using Xero for our company. First, they are not able to integrate with the credit union that we use for our checking/savings accounts. The other downside is that they do not have the option of setting up a finance charge for late payments on sales invoices. We have to manually calculate that finance charge and add it. These two things would not keep me from using Xero but it would save time if they could get those resolved.

likes:
What I like most about Xero is how intuitive and easy it is to use day-to-day. The interface is clean and straightforward, so both finance teams and non-finance staff can pick it up quickly without loads of training. Bank feeds and reconciliations save a huge amount of time, and the real-time reporting makes it easy to keep an eye on cash flow and performance. I also like the flexibility of the integrations, it connects well with lots of other systems which makes it easier to build a finance tech stack that suits the business.

From a performance perspective it’s very reliable and responsive, even when dealing with large volumes of transactions. In terms of pricing and ROI, the time savings from automation, bank feeds, and streamlined processes easily justify the cost compared with more manual systems.

Support and onboarding have also been strong. The help centre is extensive, and it’s generally easy to find guidance when you need it. For teams moving from other accounting systems, the onboarding process is straightforward and well documented.

I’ve also started to see the benefits of the AI and intelligent features, such as smart bank reconciliation suggestions and automated data capture, which continue to reduce manual work and improve accuracy over time.
dislikes:
To be honest there isn’t much to dislike. Overall it’s a very strong platform and works really well for our day-to-day finance operations. If I had to pick something, it would be that some of the more advanced reporting or customisation features can take a bit of time to configure if you’re trying to build very specific reports. Occasionally certain workflows require an add-on integration, but the marketplace of apps is large so there’s usually a solution available.

These are fairly minor points though, overall the usability, automation, and visibility Xero provides far outweigh any small limitations.
use_case:
Xero solves a lot of the manual and time-consuming aspects of finance management. Bank feeds and automated reconciliation remove a huge amount of data entry, which saves time and reduces the risk of errors. Having real-time financial data also makes it much easier to monitor cash flow, track performance, and make informed decisions without waiting for month-end reports.

It also improves collaboration. Because everything is cloud-based, our finance team, management, and external accountants can all access the same information when needed, which speeds up queries and keeps everyone aligned. Overall it streamlines financial processes, improves visibility, and frees up time to focus on more valuable work rather than admin. Intuitive, Reliable Accounting with Powerful Automation and

switched_from.reason:
We moved away from Pegasus Opera because we wanted a more modern, cloud-based finance platform. Opera had served us well for years, but it relied on more manual processes and on-premise access, which reduced flexibility. Switching to Xero has let us automate many routine tasks, including bank feeds and reconciliations, and it gives us real-time visibility of our financial data from anywhere.

The cloud-based setup has also made collaboration far easier. Our finance team, management, and external advisors can all work from the same live information without needing VPN access or installing software. Overall, the move has streamlined our processes, cut down manual work, and made reporting and financial oversight much more efficient.

Setting up Xero was also easy to follow due to its instructions.
dislikes: One downside of Xero is that some features rely on third-party add-ons, which can increase costs and complexity. Reporting options can also be somewhat limited for more advanced needs, and occasional bank feed delays can affect real-time accuracy.
use_case: Xero solves the problem of manual and time-consuming accounting processes by automating tasks like invoicing, bank reconciliation, and expense tracking. This benefits me by saving time, reducing errors, and giving real-time visibility of financial data, which helps me make quicker and more accurate decisions. Efficient Accounting with Room for

likes:
I work as a freelance account specialist and handle accounts for clients from different industries, including those selling products and services. Most of my clients prefer using Xero, and it is also the tool I feel most comfortable using in my day-to-day work.

Xero is very easy to use, and I was able to learn it quickly compared to other tools. It is also easy to implement into my workflow since it fits well with the way I manage different clients and Xero's client tab is very easy to vaiate. I use it frequently, and it helps me stay organized across multiple accounts.

The bank feed feature is one of the main reasons I like Xero, as the automated bank reconciliations save a lot of time and reduce manual work. The reporting features are also very useful for tracking and analyzing financial data for different clients.

Another advantage is that Xero integrates well with other tools, which makes it easier to manage everything in one place. Earlier in my career, I used to handle many of these tasks manually, so Xero has made my work much more efficient and easier to manage.

I also find the learning resources helpful. The course videos made it easier for me to understand the platform and improve how I use it. During my pro advisor certificate exam, I was required to reach the customer service and they are pretty fast.
dislikes: One thing I find a bit inconvenient is that the partner program can be deactivated if you don’t have any clients to add. That said, I do appreciate that you can rejoin it at any time, which gives you some added flexibility.
use_case:
Before using Xero, I often relied on spreadsheets to handle my accounting tasks. Although spreadsheets are flexible, they involve a lot of manual effort. I used to spend a significant amount of time entering transactions, making journal entries, updating records, and building reports by hand, which slowed everything down and increased the chance of mistakes.

Xero addresses many of these issues by automating key parts of the workflow. For instance, the bank feed feature imports transactions directly from the bank, so I don’t have to enter everything manually. This saves a lot of time and makes the overall process much more efficient.

The automated bank reconciliation also makes sure transactions are recorded accurately. With spreadsheets, even a small formula error can affect the entire file, but in Xero the system follows proper accounting rules. That makes the data more reliable and helps reduce errors.

Xero also supports invoicing and client payments by sending automatic reminders for overdue invoices. This reduces the need to chase payments manually and can help improve cash flow.

Another advantage is collaboration. Clients can grant access based on what’s needed, and participants can upload documents easily without disrupting the accounting records. Xero Makes Bookkeeping Effortless with Time-Saving Bank

likes: I like the reduction of cognitive load with Xero. Unlike old accounting software, where I had to think like a computer, Xero allows me to think like a business owner while it translates into accounting language in the background. The AI-powered data capture and JAX create records for me in under 20 seconds, which moves my brain into Audit Mode, making it much less taxing to verify transactions than to build them from scratch. I also find the initial onboarding to be beautifully designed, reminiscent of an Apple experience, allowing me to be up and running in just 15 minutes.
dislikes: Small employers feel hijacked. Many reported their monthly bills doubling or tripling just to keep 2 employees compliant with STP. Previously, a small business owner paying themselves could use the $32/month starter plan. Now, they must move to the higher plan at $75/month just to pay themselves and be compliant with the STP. The 'Apple' analogy holds true here, the onboarding is beautiful, but the infrastructure requires professional attention. Xero's initial setup is a choose your own adventure experience. You can be up and running in 15 minutes, but being tax-ready takes a few hours of focused work.

likes: I really like all the integrations in Xero, especially the native ones with different financial accounts and robust Zapier integrations that allow me to automate a lot of my daily workflow. The ability to build automations with Zapier is a big one for me, as it can identify invoices and receipts sent by email, and then automatically create a new bill in Xero or attach the receipt to a relevant transaction if it's already reconciled. I also use Zapier to sync my sales invoices to my CRM in Notion, so I have visibility of all invoices and their current status. I found that setting up Xero was quite easy for me since I didn't have any previous financials to manage, and I particularly liked the refreshed homepage.
dislikes: Working directly with the API is a little bit challenging. Some of the syntax feels a little bit outdated. Querying for a transaction is pretty difficult. Some parts of the UI feel very dated, with the settings and reporting screens still feeling like they're from the early 2000s.

likes:
What I like most about Xero is how easy it is to use. It doesn’t feel complicated or overwhelming, even when you’re dealing with more detailed finance tasks. Everything is quite intuitive, which saves a lot of time day to day. I also really like how clear and convenient the reports are - you can quickly get the numbers you need without spending ages clicking around. And having full visibility of the history of actions is a big plus. Being able to see who did what and when gives real transparency and makes it easier to stay in control. I also recently talked to customer support and they resolved my issue quickly!

Overall, it just makes my work feel more organised and efficient, it's easy to implement and integrate to our other tools.
dislikes:
What I dislike about Xero is that some options feel a bit limited or missing.

For example, I wish there was a way to send automatic monthly statements to customers without setting up workarounds. It would also be really useful if customers could log in and download their up-to-date statements themselves whenever they need them.

Same with invoice reminders - the settings are quite basic. In reality, there are different types of invoices that need different reminder flows, and it would be great to have more flexibility there instead of one standard setup.

So overall it works well, but a few more advanced AR features would make a big difference.
use_case:
It takes care of a lot of day-to-day finance admin and keeps everything in one place, which genuinely makes a big difference.

For me, the main benefit is having clear, end-to-end visibility of invoices, payments, and account balances without needing to pull information from multiple sources. That makes it much easier to track outstanding amounts, reconcile payments, and follow up on overdue invoices.

It’s also strong from a control and audit perspective. Having a clear history of actions, payment allocations, and changes reduces confusion and saves time when I need to investigate issues or understand what happened.

Overall, it streamlines processes that would otherwise be manual and time-consuming, so I can spend more time on analysis and problem-solving rather than admin work. Intuitive, Time-Saving Accounting with Clear Reports and Full

dislikes: As payments get more complicated and there are delays in getting paid, Xero needs to update the pending status from Stripe to better indicate whether a payment is pending, paid, or not paid. likes: Running a restaurant means juggling a thousand moving parts, and Xero has been one of the few tools that actually removes stress instead of adding to it. What I appreciate most is how smoothly it handles the daily financial flow, bank feeds pull in every sale and supplier charge without me chasing numbers, and reconciling feels almost effortless compared to tools I’ve used before. The interface is clean and intuitive, which matters when you’re switching between recipe testing, staff questions, and invoices in the same hour. Integrations with food-cost and inventory apps also make it easier to keep an eye on margins, something that’s become essential in the F&B world. It just feels like a system built for people who don’t have time to “figure out” software every day.
dislikes: If I had to point at something, it’s the limits around reports. As someone who likes digging deeper into food cost fluctuations and supplier trends, the reporting templates feel a bit rigid. Sometimes I end up exporting things just to get the view I need, which interrupts the otherwise smooth workflow. And while Xero integrates well with many tools, a few restaurant-specific add-ons still require extra setup or fine-tuning before everything syncs the way I want. None of this stops me from using it, but I do notice those friction points in busy weeks.
use_case: Xero gives me back time, real time, which is probably the rarest ingredient in this industry. Instead of drowning in spreadsheets after service, I get a clear picture of cash flow, bills, payroll, and inventory movement in minutes. It also supports the way I work: hands-on, always evolving, always planning for the next concept or challenge. When the financial backend runs smoothly, I can focus on creativity, staff, food, and the bigger vision. It’s helped me build a more reliable structure behind the restaurant, and that structure is what makes growth, even franchising, actually possible.

likes:
What I like best about Xero is how simple and user-friendly it is, even for someone without a strong accounting background. The dashboard gives a clear snapshot of cash flow, invoices, and expenses, making it easy to stay on top of finances at a glance.

The most helpful feature is the automatic bank feeds and smart reconciliation. It significantly reduces manual data entry and saves a lot of time. Creating and sending invoices is quick, and tracking payments is straightforward.

The biggest upsides of using Xero are its cloud-based access, strong reporting tools, and seamless integration with other business apps. It makes collaboration with accountants smooth and keeps everything organized in one place.
dislikes:
One downside of Xero is that some advanced features can feel overwhelming at first, especially for new users without accounting experience. While the interface is clean, there can still be a learning curve when navigating more complex reports or settings.

Another limitation is pricing. As your business grows and you need additional features like payroll or multi-currency support, the subscription cost can increase significantly. Some integrations also require separate paid subscriptions.

Additionally, customer support response times can sometimes be slower than expected, particularly during busy periods. While the help center is detailed, direct support isn’t always immediate.
use_case:
Xero is solving the problem of manual, time-consuming bookkeeping and lack of real-time financial visibility. Before using it, tracking invoices, expenses, and bank transactions required a lot of spreadsheets and manual reconciliation, which increased the risk of errors.

Xero automates bank feeds, transaction matching, and invoice tracking, which saves significant time and improves accuracy. It also provides real-time financial insights, helping me monitor cash flow, outstanding payments, and overall business performance more effectively.

By centralizing accounting, reporting, and collaboration with accountants in one cloud-based system, Xero reduces administrative stress and allows me to focus more on core business activities instead of paperwork. Efficient, User-Friendly

likes: I find Xero's intuitive interface incredibly helpful for recording all financial transactions accurately, making it easy to log sales, purchases, and expenses. The automation of bookkeeping tasks, like posting transactions to the general ledger, keeps my financial records up-to-date without manual effort. Bank reconciliation is a lot simpler because of Xero's ability to connect with business bank accounts and automatically import transactions, saving me time and helping catch discrepancies quickly. Creating professional invoices is straightforward; I love how customizable they are with logos and branding, plus setting up recurring invoices is no hassle. Xero's features for payment collection, including automated payment reminders, make managing cash flow a lot easier by reducing late payments. Real-time financial reporting is a huge advantage for making informed business decisions, offering immediate insights and custom reports for in-depth analysis, which helps my team understand the financial impact of different strategies effectively.
dislikes: Integration Challenges: While Xero integrates with many popular third-party applications, integrating with niche or industry-specific software can be problematic. For example, in the manufacturing industry, there are specialized inventory management systems that have unique data structures and requirements. Integrating Xero with these systems often requires significant technical expertise and may result in inconsistent data transfer. This can disrupt the seamless flow of information between different business processes, leading to inefficiencies and potential errors in financial reporting. API Limitations: For developers or businesses looking to build custom integrations, Xero's API has some restrictions. The available API endpoints may not cover all aspects of Xero's functionality. This limits the development of more comprehensive and tailored integrations. For instance, if a business wants to create a custom application that interacts with Xero's advanced accounting workflows, the lack of complete API access can be a major bottleneck.
use_case: Xero automates data entry by importing transactions directly, reducing errors and saving time. It simplifies invoicing and billing with customizable templates and automated reminders. Real-time financial data provides crucial insights for decision-making, while customizable reports allow in-depth financial analysis, improving cash flow management and strategic planning. Streamlined Bookkeeping,

likes: I really find Xero's interface to be very user-friendly and easy to use, both for a team and individually. This user-friendliness stands out to me and makes a big difference when working with the platform.
dislikes: I genuinely think that there are two things that could be improved. One of them is the spreadsheet stuff. Like how the data is answered and how it's shown to the user of Xero; it doesn't kinda sit well with me at the moment. The support quality could also be improved because sometimes the responses get a bit too slow.
use_case: Xero automates our finance management, saving us time and headaches. It handles our financial data, including reporting and analysis features, which would be time-consuming to do manually. Time-Saving, User-Friendly Yet Needs

likes: I find Xero easy to use, which is great for managing invoicing and accounting for my business. The Support section is very helpful, especially when my bank changes its interface. The support articles provide excellent step-by-step instructions. It’s also convenient that my CPA can log in to access ledgers and reports for tax preparation. I appreciate how it makes creating and sending invoices and reconciling with my bank so straightforward. Overall, Xero efficiently logs my expenses and supports my business needs.
dislikes: I don't have any complaints
use_case: I find Xero makes invoicing easy, handles bank reconciliation and expense logging efficiently, and provides CPA access for tax prep. The Support section is helpful for resolving issues like bank feed changes. Effortless Invoicing and

dislikes: Sometimes, organizing expenses according to categories may be somewhat difficult.

likes: Xero catches things I’d forget in the rush of pop ups and prep days. My bank feed updates on its own, which feels like someone quietly tidying the counter while I’m busy stirring something else. Other people mention how simple and reliable it is, and I feel the same. Sending invoices with my own branding also makes me oddly happy. It looks like Eating Fantasy, not some generic template, and payments show up right away, which takes a load off.
dislikes: I haven’t hit any major frustrations yet. Till now just working fine.
use_case: Xero gives me a bit of stability in the middle of all the moving parts of Eating Fantasy. I’m still new to running a business, so having something that quietly keeps my sales, expenses, and invoices organized feels grounding. My pop up income shows up automatically, the invoices look like my brand, and I don’t have to double check every little thing. It helps me stay aware of where I stand without pulling me away from cooking or planning the next event. It’s simple, but it makes the whole operation feel more manageable and real.

bonus_data.title: Xero helps me keep the business side organized so I can cook

likes: Xero is easy to use, visually clean, and great for small businesses like ours. I especially like how fast I can generate and send invoices, reconcile bank transactions, and view real-time financial reports. The Singapore payroll integration with CPF is also a huge plus.
dislikes: The mobile app is not as powerful as the desktop version, especially for payroll and bank reconciliation. Also, some advanced features like multi-currency reporting could be more intuitive or flexible for small businesses.
use_case: Xero helps us manage invoicing, expense tracking, payroll, and CPF contributions all in one place. It has significantly reduced manual errors and saved us time compared to using spreadsheets. The real-time view of cash flow and bank reconciliation is especially helpful for decision-making in a small business like ours. Effortless Accounting and Invoicing for

likes:
I enjoy about Xero is how it settles into the background of my work without creating extra noise. Our days at ViralMint get pretty packed with campaign changes, client calls, and the usual scramble that comes with performance marketing, so having something that quietly keeps the books in line feels like a relief.
Sending invoices is quick, the bank feeds match up almost on their own, and I don’t find myself hunting through old sheets or digging through email trails to confirm what got paid. The interface feels simple enough that I don’t have to explain it twice to anyone on the team. It almost feels like the finance part of the business finally behaves the way we wish the rest of our tools did, steady and predictable.
dislikes: The mobile app also feels a little thin. It’s fine when I’m traveling and just want to check a payment, but if I need to actually work on something, I wait until I’m back on my laptop. And during crunch times, when we’re handling audits or heavy reconciliation, the support responses can feel slower than we’d like. It’s not terrible, just one of those small frictions that stick in your mind when you’re already juggling too much.
use_case:
Xero has made our financial workflow feel cleaner, almost calmer. We handle a lot of ad spend, vendor invoices, client billing cycles, all moving at their own pace, and having a single place where everything lands makes a big difference.
It helps us see cash flow without guessing. Invoices go out faster, reconciliation doesn’t turn into a full-day task, and tax time isn’t the chaotic mess it used to be. It frees up space in my head so I can stay focused on the actual work we do for ecommerce brands, things like optimizing funnels or scaling campaigns, instead of worrying about where a payment went or whether something will get missed later.
It’s not perfect, but it does the job in a way that makes running the business feel more grounded. And that matters more than anything.

bonus_data.title: Xero keeps our financial side steady so the marketing work can keep moving

switched_from.reason: We were running almost everything on Excel. It worked for a while, or at least we convinced ourselves it did. But once our client list started growing and the number of campaigns we managed each month began to pile up, the sheets just couldn’t keep up.

likes: I like Xero for its overall simplicity, especially compared to QuickBooks Online. I find the invoicing feature and Stripe integration in Xero very valuable. Setting up Xero was really quick and easy, and it's one of the easiest products to use, also better compared to QuickBooks.
dislikes: The ability to format the emails that proceed the invoicing could be improved. Being able to format them more like the brand-oriented invoicing would be better.

likes: It’s easy to set up and integrate, the features that Xero incorporates each update are genuinely product requests from the users. The developers and designers understand the need to reduce workflow steps by simplifying menu items and keeping things accessible at a glance.
dislikes: Nothing major, the need to press "More Details" every time I want to read more than half the bank transaction description is irritating. The popup also doesn't just disappear when you 'step off' the window. Also, similar to other online accounting cloud software you cannot be working on more than one client company at a time.
use_case: Intuitive cloud-based accounting software with a team dedicated to improvements.

likes: I think the reporting features in Xero are the best, especially the custom reporting. It's really easy for clients to use, and it's straightforward for new individuals being onboarded. Compared to competitors, it's a way better tool since clients can use it easier. I'm really looking forward to the AI functions this year.
dislikes: A lot of the work in progress items don't get saved automatically. Sometimes your finger slips, and then you lose all of your progress. That's been not great. I'm hoping it can expand a bit more on custom reporting, automations, and grouping. GL account groupings and things that enable better consolidation, forecasting tools, and cash flow forecasting would interest me a lot.

dislikes: I still don't understand the Xero balance vs Bank Account balances, reconciliation is a bit confusing. I don't know why there are two balances in the first place. What is the purpose of having a bank account balance and a Xero balance? The bank account should be the system of record / truth. As for reconciliation, the process is somewhat straightforward but again the amounts are different so it makes it hard to understand and have true reconciliation periods.
use_case: Xero helps with invoicing customers, tracking payments, and managing AR/AP. Its easy navigation makes sending invoices and tracking payments straightforward. Efficient Invoicing, Needs Balance

likes: I love Xero. It's so clean and easy to use, yet it has all of the functionality of a much more complicated system. The integrations work flawlessly, especially compared to competitors. I appreciate how app integrations can be customized for each client's needs, making it more powerful than others. My clients can actually use and understand it fairly well on a daily basis. It provides me with all the higher-level information and ledgers and reports that I need to do my job and help prepare taxes. I use integrations like Hubdoc and Syft, which are a huge help and time saver, and having it all in one place is fantastic. Integrations with Xero work without the need for extra steps, which is very helpful for my clients and me in running businesses.
dislikes: Customer service can be frustrating because I prefer to call rather than email. Also, there is an issue with pulling data directly from Google Sheets as a purchase or sales order, which my client finds to be a problem, however this is probably solved with another app integration.
use_case: I use Xero for its clean, easy-to-use interface and robust integrations that are powerful yet simple for clients. It efficiently manages data, ledgers, and reports essential for tax preparation, saving time and helping clients run their businesses. Seamless Integrations, Unmatched

likes: I find Xero's streamlined approach makes accounting much easier. I like that it helps us manage our finances as we scale. Chargebee directly pushes invoice and payment data into Xero, categorizing it correctly, which helps us stay on top of our accounting without needing to track everything manually. Setting up Xero was very simple, and I'm very likely to recommend it.
dislikes: Makes accounting almost fun!

likes: I find Xero more user-friendly than other accounting software I've used, and it doesn't seem to have hiccups as often. It's also less expensive. likes: Xero is a solid, no-nonsense accounting tool. Its best feature is its clean, modern user interface, which is generally easy to navigate for daily tasks. The bank feed and reconciliation feature, in particular, is very well done and makes a complex task feel simple and fast. For all core accounting functions like invoicing and managing bills, it's reliable and gets the job done without much fuss.
dislikes: Our main frustrations come from its rigidity, especially with integrations. The API and integration options feel limited and aren't as flexible as other modern platforms. We had to invest in significant custom development work just to get it to connect properly with our other critical systems. Beyond that, the reporting features feel very rigid, making it difficult to get customized reports, and finding fast, direct customer support can also be a challenge.
use_case: Xero solves the fundamental problem of financial disorganization. As a multinational company, we have complex needs, and Xero provides a single, centralized platform for all our core accounting, billing, and payroll. The main benefit is having a single source of truth for our finances. It allows us to manage expenses, send invoices, and run payroll in one place, which is essential for staying compliant and understanding our financial health. The Finance Team's

switched_from.reason: The move from NetSuite to Xero was almost certainly driven by a strategic shift away from cost and complexity toward usability and flexibility. NetSuite is a massive, all-in-one ERP that is incredibly powerful but also extremely expensive, often requiring specialized consultants. Xero is significantly more cost-effective and aligns far better with an agile company culture like Voys's. This isn't to contradict my earlier points; "simpler" doesn't mean "simple." All powerful finance tools, including Xero, still require "know-how" and have a real learning curve. But from a company perspective, the change was about moving from a single, rigid, monolithic system (NetSuite) to a more flexible "best-of-breed" stack

likes: I really like Xero for the reporting and dashboard features. The integration with Syft for deeper analytics is also helpful for both my business and my clients.
dislikes: I wish Xero integrated with more third party providers. The list of integrations is growing on a regular basis, and I am eagerly awaiting additional third party integrations.

I also like that I don't have to do too much more for my accountant at the end of the financial year either - I save a lot of money by doing my own recon which as a small business owner is important to me.

likes: When we started DigitalRyze, I didn’t think much about accounting software. It felt like one of those things you figure out later. But once we started using Xero, I realized how much mental space it saves. Sending invoices to clients in the UK or Canada takes just a few clicks now, and it looks clean and professional, no awkward PDFs or messy spreadsheets. What I really appreciate is the way it keeps everything connected. The moment a client pays, it syncs through Stripe, and I can see it reflected in my dashboard almost instantly. There’s a small sense of satisfaction in watching the numbers line up, knowing that I don’t have to dig through my email to confirm payments.
dislikes: There are a few things that still feel a bit stiff. Some of the deeper reports take a while to figure out, and sometimes I end up Googling how to do something that should be obvious. The mobile app is fine for checking numbers or sending a quick invoice, but it’s not great for more detailed work. I wish it matched the desktop version more closely. Small frustrations, but they add up when you’re on the move a lot.
use_case:
Xero kind of gave us structure without making it feel like we were being boxed in. Before it, I was constantly switching between Excel sheets and WhatsApp messages to track who paid what. Now everything lives in one place, which means I can actually focus on growing the business instead of chasing paperwork. It’s also made it easier to work with clients abroad, no confusion about currencies or due dates, no awkward follow-ups.
It’s funny, but Xero doesn’t feel like “software” to me anymore. It’s just part of how we run things. Quietly in the background, keeping our finances clean so I can keep my head in the creative work. That’s probably the best compliment I can give it. Xero quietly became one of those tools

use_case: Xero doesn't charge per user which is helpful financially for us, it is a rare move in the SaaS world today. Best Accounting Tool with Clean UI and

likes: What I appreciate most about Xero is its ability to combine robust features with straightforward usability. The interface is clean and intuitive, making it one of the most approachable accounting platforms, particularly for those without an accounting background. Real-time bank feeds and automatic reconciliation significantly cut down on manual work and help minimize errors. Xero’s strong ecosystem stands out as well—the app marketplace integrates seamlessly with tools for payroll, inventory, payments, CRM, and reporting. Collaboration is smooth, allowing multiple users such as accountants, bookkeepers, and business owners to work together simultaneously without any hassle. The reporting features provide clear visibility, with cash flow, profit and loss, and balance sheets all easy to find and interpret. Being cloud-based means you can access your work from anywhere, and updates are applied without any disruption.
dislikes: There are a few aspects of Xero that I find less appealing, despite it being a robust platform overall. The inventory management features are quite limited—while they work for straightforward products, they fall short for businesses dealing with complex stock, assemblies, or detailed cost tracking. Customizing reports can also be a challenge; although the reporting tools are powerful once you get the hang of them, creating truly personalized reports isn’t very intuitive. Payroll functionality varies by region—while it performs well in some countries, in others it’s quite basic or requires the use of third-party applications. I’ve also noticed that pricing can escalate quickly, especially as your needs change or you start depending on paid integrations. Some manual work is still necessary, as bank rules and reconciliations aren’t as automated as I’d like. Lastly, the support model could be improved; there’s no phone support, so you have to rely on tickets and help articles, which can be slow when you need urgent assistance.
use_case: Traditional bookkeeping often feels outdated and requires a lot of manual work. This software automates tasks like bank feeds, reconciliations, and invoicing, which helps keep the books up to date without the need for constant data entry. One major issue with older systems is the lack of financial visibility, but here, you get real-time access to cash flow, profit, and liabilities, so there are no more unexpected surprises at the end of the month. Collaboration between business owners and accountants can also be a challenge, but this platform allows multiple users to work together in the same system at the same time, with clear audit trails for accountability. Another common problem is having disconnected business tools, but this solution integrates accounting with payroll, payments, inventory, and reporting, ensuring that data moves seamlessly between them. Finally, for those who feel intimidated or confused by accounting, the use of plain language and a clean user interface makes it much more approachable for people without a financial background. Effortless Accounting with Powerful Features and Seamless

likes: As a business analyst, what I appreciate most about Xero is the real-time visibility it provides into financial data. Having live dashboards for cash flow, invoices, and expenses gives me a reliable starting point for deeper analysis. The bank feeds and automatic reconciliations ensure that the data I work with is accurate and up to date. I also like how Xero integrates with Power BI, Excel, and Google Sheets, which allows me to build customized reports and dashboards tailored to leadership's needs. This integration reduces dependency on manual data pulls and helps accelerate decision-making. but overall its easy to use.
dislikes: While it handles standard reporting well, it does not always provide the level of customization and granularity I need as an analyst. For example, creating cross-entity consolidated reports or drilling down by multiple custom dimensions often requires exporting data into BI tools. The forecasting capabilities are helpful, but they are limited when it comes to more advanced scenario planning, so I still need to rely on external models. Additionally, working with larger datasets can feel a bit slow, especially when running queries or exporting data.
use_case: Xero has helped me significantly reduce the time I spend on data preparation. Instead of manually reconciling transactions or cleaning up messy spreadsheets, I can now rely on Xero's automation features to provide cleaner datasets. This allows me to dedicate more time to analyzing insights, such as identifying cost-saving opportunities or spotting revenue trends, rather than constantly fixing errors. It also enhances collaboration with finance and business development, as we are all working from a single, consistent source of truth. The platform enables faster financial reporting and variance analysis, as well as more accurate cash flow monitoring and forecasting. Additionally, it supports better analysis of revenue and expense trends to inform strategy and fosters closer alignment between the finance and business teams.

bonus_data.title: Strong financial visibility tool but advanced analytics still needed external support

likes: I find Xero incredibly valuable for accounting, as it serves as a comprehensive tool for accountants, encompassing everything from bookkeeping to the preparation of Business Activity Statements (BAS) and tax filing. I appreciate that Xero makes the whole bookkeeping and tax preparation journey seamless and organized, which is crucial in my line of work.
dislikes: I find that the workpaper packs in Xero aren't as advanced or helpful as they could be. They need to be more user-friendly and incorporate advanced features such as AI and automation to enhance efficiency in creating workpapers. Additionally, the workpaper packs could benefit from a checklist section to verify necessary tax filing requirements and an easier way to link documents to a shared drive, which would streamline the process. The visual aspect of the workpaper packs could also be more appealing, and there should be better support for adding multiple users, such as preparers, users, and reviewers. Lastly, I feel there should be a stricter emphasis on tax preparation with better linkages to Xero Practice Manager, enabling seamless integration of figures like franking accounts and depreciation.

I am also impressed enough with Xero that I would rate my likelihood of recommending it to a friend or colleague as a ten out of ten.

Sending bills via the Xero network effectively, especially with attachments, enhances my experience greatly. I also appreciate the seamless integration with other tools like Gusto and Veem, and the initial setup was very easy. The system is efficient for capturing monthly depreciation and pulling tailored reports, making it incredibly organized and easy to manage, even without a strong accounting background. I like its simplicity and user-friendliness, which keeps everything well-organized. Another feature I find valuable is the ability to share documents like invoices, statements, purchase orders, and payment remittances directly with third parties without needing to download or resend them via email, speeding up communication and making it more convenient. My team and I use this feature almost daily. The initial setup of Xero was easy and hassle-free, enhancing my overall satisfaction with the product.
dislikes: I have an account that was locked and accidentally deleted. The balance still appears in my balance sheet, and I am unable to clear or update it. I am not sure how to rectify this. In both cases, Xero product teams simply closed the feature requests and said they will not review customer feedback on pricing/plans. Xero also doesn’t support bank feeds for my bank CDs (though my bank does), or for Venmo accounts. I wish Xero spent more time on basic customer feedback like this, rather than AI washing their product marketing.
