likes: I appreciate Xero for its simplicity and ease of setup, which was a significant improvement over my previous experience with QuickBooks. The step-by-step guidance made the transition smooth, and finding help was straightforward whenever I encountered challenges. One of Xero's most valuable features for my business is the ability to automatically connect and update bank and credit card feeds. This live update feature allows me to reconcile accounts efficiently, which is crucial for tracking business finances without the need for manual entries or spreadsheets. I also value the cost-effectiveness of Xero, as its price point is much lower than my former software, and the initial discount offered was fair and greatly appreciated. Moreover, Xero's customer support is responsive and helpful, making it easy to submit tickets and receive prompt, step-by-step assistance to resolve issues, enhancing my overall satisfaction with the product.
dislikes: I find that Xero does not always connect well with my bank, especially since I am using smaller, local banks as opposed to large national banks. This has caused some connectivity issues in the past. Additionally, I don't think we're utilizing all of Xero's features to their full potential, partly because we're not aware of all the capabilities it offers.

likes: Logical layout greatly helped by the new navigation structure. Relatively easy to learn and I have learned quite a few systems now. Excellent world wide tech support. Particularly useful during the initial phases.
dislikes: I really appreciate the find and match feature for bank reconciliation. However, it would be much more useful if it allowed reconciliation to clearing accounts as well. In my workflow, I use Jane EMR as the customer front end, which processes credit card payments and marks invoices as paid. The issue is that the funds are not deposited immediately; there is a delay of several days. To prevent problems at month end, I record these credit card payments in a clearing account. When the payments eventually arrive in the bank account as a batch, I reconcile them to the clearing account, but I have to do this manually because the find and match feature does not support it. If the intention is to keep 'Find and Match' uncluttered, perhaps there could be a separate function, such as 'Find and Match Clearing,' where you can specify an account to match from.
use_case: As an accounting program, it assists me in preparing the monthly accounts for a Physical Therapy business. It streamlines the process significantly and ensures that I remain compliant with the necessary guidelines. Easy to Learn Accounting

The snap and scan feature stands out as a particularly useful capability, allowing employees to take pictures of receipts for automatic analysis, thereby eliminating the need for manual entry. likes:
Xero stands out for making daily finance tasks less of a headache, even for those without an accounting background

Invoicing and expense tracking are genuinely easy, and the real-time dashboard gives a clear view of where the business stands at any momenT

 The cloud-based access is a time-saver, letting everyone—from business owners to finance heads—work from anywhere and collaborate smoothly. 

Integrations with tools like Stripe and Gusto also help avoid manual data entry, and the automation of reminders and invoice emails is a quiet game-changer for chasing payments
dislikes:
While Xero does make day-to-day work easier, not everything is perfect. The interface feels a bit dated and sometimes clunky, especially for more complex accounting or reporting tasks

The reporting options are underwhelming—you can’t really customize them much, which can feel limiting if you want to present data in a more visual or tailored way
use_case: By automating things like invoice reminders, bank feeds, and expense categorization, it removes a lot of manual grunt work, helping keep financial records in order and freeing up more time to actually run the business

likes:
As a firm of Chartered Accountants we recommend Xero to all our clients.

With Making Tax Digital for Income Tax being introduced in April 2026 digital software will be essential for sole traders and we consider Xero to be the go to software.

Our clients who use Xero are very happy with the software and we find that it greatly assists us in providing a complete service to our clients.

Overall some very good software.
dislikes:
Xero is very good software for what it does.

Unlike many software supplies they do not provide telephone support to their customers. Any queries are recorded online and then Xero get back to the person making the query via their online portal. Generally the support is knowledgeable but due to the time taken to reply (from 2 hours to 24 hours) if you are needing an urgent reply this can be frustrating.

Xero have over the years brought in a number of what they consider to be enhancements to their core bookkeeping product. These "enhancements" do not always provide a better experience for their users.

Recently there was a change in their invoicing package. The changes made did not seem to be in consultation with any users and there were so many complaints that they had to delay withdrawing the old package. Many utilities of the old invoicing system which were liked by users were not carried forward and there have been hundreds of complaints on the online forums requesting some of the old functionality to be reinstated.

Xero's response has been very much " we hear what you say but we are going to do nothing about it" .

This does not foster good relationships with users. I know of a number of accountancy firms who are fed up and have moved or are looking to move their clients to other bookkeeping packages.

There a regular price increases which do not reflect better usage or improvement to the overall software and again I know of practices who consider that Xero are pricing themselves out of the lucrative market they operate in.

Overall Xero need to listen and react better to their customer base.
use_case: Xero is a good online bookkeeping system that enables bookkeeping and invoicing to be carried out quickly and smoothly. With the upcoming implementation of MTD SA it will be very useful to be able to make submissions to HMRC as required by the legislation.

bonus_data.title: Good software but they do not take accoutn of their users needs in full

likes:
Xero makes accounting genuinely simple even for users who don’t come from a traditional finance background. The interface is clean, intuitive, and incredibly easy to navigate. Everything from bank feeds to invoicing to reconciliation just works seamlessly, right out of the box.

The onboarding process was smooth we were fully up and running within a couple of days, without needing much support or training. Xero has become an integral part of our daily workflow, especially for reconciling transactions, managing cash flow, and tracking expenses.

One of the biggest advantages is the automation it significantly reduces manual work and gives us real-time updates that help with faster decision-making and team collaboration.

Support has also been excellent whenever we’ve needed help, we've received quick responses, helpful guidance, and easy-to-follow documentation. The integrations (especially with Payhawk and our payroll platforms) have worked smoothly, syncing data with no extra effort.

Overall, Xero has made our finance operations more efficient, more transparent, and a lot less stressful.
dislikes:
There isn’t much to complain about with Xero it’s a very well-built platform. But if I had to point something out, it would be that some of the reporting features could be more flexible. While the standard reports cover most use cases, building customised or more complex reports sometimes requires third-party add-ons or workarounds, which could be streamlined further.

Also, the search function can feel a bit limited, especially when you're trying to locate older transactions or drill down to specific line items. A more advanced filter or search logic would make a big difference.

That said, these are minor issues in the grand scheme of things. The platform’s core features reconciliation, invoicing, and bank syncing work reliably and save us a lot of time.
use_case:
Xero has removed a lot of the manual work, confusion, and back-and-forth that used to slow down our finance operations. We’re now able to stay on top of payables, receivables, and bank reconciliations in real time without digging through spreadsheets or chasing email threads for missing info.

The real-time visibility into our cash flow and financial position has been a game-changer for planning and decision-making. At any point, we can see where things stand and make informed calls, which has brought more confidence to our budgeting and forecasting.

A major pain point Xero solved for us is team collaboration. Since it's cloud-based, our entire finance, ops, and accounting teams can work in Xero simultaneously no version-control issues or delays. This has especially helped during month-end close and audit preparation, where speed and accuracy matter most.

The automation, clean workflows, and accuracy Xero brings have saved us hours of manual work every week, reduced the chance of human error, and given us a system we can truly rely on. Xero Has Streamlined Bookkeeping, Invoicing, and Expense

switched_from.reason:
We were using Microsoft Dynamics 365 before Xero, but as a growing company, we found Dynamics a bit too complex and heavy for our needs. While it’s a powerful ERP solution, it felt like overkill for our day-to-day accounting workflows and it often required dedicated IT resources or consultants to set things up or fix issues.

Xero, by contrast, is much more user-friendly and intuitive, especially for small to mid-sized teams. It doesn’t require any technical background to operate, and the onboarding was quick and self-serve. Within a couple of days, we were fully operational.

In terms of functionality, Xero covers all the essentials bank reconciliation, invoicing, accounts payable/receivable, reporting, and tax prep without the overhead that came with Dynamics. Also, the integrations with other tools like Payhawk, payroll platforms, and Stripe have worked flawlessly.

Overall, switching to Xero has made our finance processes simpler, more efficient, and far easier to manage and it’s been a better fit for our current scale and team setup.

likes:
What I like best about Xero is its simplicity and smart design. Even if you're not from an accounting background, the interface is intuitive and easy to navigate. It doesn't overwhelm you with technical jargon or cluttered dashboards—everything is clean, straightforward, and just works.

The most helpful part of Xero is how seamlessly it connects everything. From bank feeds and invoicing to expense tracking and reconciliation, it’s all automated and integrated beautifully. I especially love the live bank feed—it saves hours of manual work and ensures my books are always up to date in real time.

The upsides of using Xero are countless. It's cloud-based, so you can work from anywhere, collaborate with your team or accountant, and even manage multiple entities without needing separate logins. The reporting features are excellent too—you get clear insights into your cash flow, P&L, and balance sheet at the click of a button.

Overall, Xero has taken the stress out of bookkeeping and turned it into a streamlined, efficient process. It helps us focus more on decision-making and less on chasing numbers—truly a game-changer for modern businesses.
dislikes:
While Xero is a fantastic tool overall, there are a few areas where there’s room for improvement.

One of the main challenges is the limited customization in reporting. While the default reports are helpful, creating more complex, tailored reports often requires workarounds or external tools. For businesses with unique reporting needs, this can be a bit limiting.

Another point is the learning curve for new users. Although the platform is user-friendly once you're familiar with it, some features—like tracking categories or setting up rules for bank reconciliation—can be slightly confusing at first without proper onboarding or tutorials.

Lastly, the customer support experience can sometimes feel a bit slow. Most support is via email or tickets, and in situations where you need quick, real-time assistance, it would be great to have access to live chat or phone support.

That said, these are relatively minor in the grand scheme of things. The core functionalities are strong, and Xero continues to improve with updates based on user feedback—which is always encouraging.
use_case:
Xero has streamlined our financial processes by automating bookkeeping, bank reconciliation, and invoicing—saving us time and reducing errors.

It gives us real-time visibility into cash flow and performance, which helps in faster, better decision-making.

By integrating everything in one place and allowing secure collaboration with our team and accountants, Xero has made financial management more efficient, connected, and stress-free. Best Accounting

dislikes: There are still a lot of non intuitive features and many parts of it are not "true accounting". I wish the analytics were better. The fixed asset register is horrible you should just be able to make a COA account type for fixed assets and then Xero should track/make entries for depreciation and accum depreciation automatically each month or year (let users decide). Xero also needs to make a better receipt tracking feature - hub doc is awful especially for people like myself with 3-5 llcs on xero making me sign in an out each time. For a great example of an amazing experince look up what Brex and Ramp do. Each time you spend money they get rich data from card processors and make an virtual copy of your receipt that the IRS accepts. You also get a text that says "please categorize this receipt" as soon as you spend so that you can do it while its fresh in your mind as you walk out of the store.

likes:
Xero *was* a well-designed and thoughtfully planned software. 
The setup process is straightforward, and it’s very easy to use day-to-day. 
Navigating through transactions or generating reports is simple and intuitive, making it accessible even for those who aren’t accountants.
dislikes:
There is a desperate lack of customer support, especially if you encounter an issue or have a complaint. 
Xero seems more focused on making the software “beautiful” rather than listening to user feedback. Many highly requested features have sat on the forums with hundreds (sometimes thousands) of votes, yet there is little to no response from Xero. On top of this, prices keep increasing—often multiple times a year. It also feels like Xero is more interested in driving revenue through third-party integrations; core features are left out so that users are pushed toward paying for additional apps via their API ecosystem.
use_case: Xero works well as a basic accounting package. It’s intuitive to use, helps us complete our quarterly BAS with minimal hassle, and gives our accountant direct access whenever we have a query or when it’s time to prepare the annual tax return. However, Xero does not provide the ability to prepare your own tax return, and with the constant price increases it becomes hard to justify. While other software may not be quite as intuitive, many offer the same end results at a much more reasonable cost.

likes: Xero has massively increased prices and added very little of value.
dislikes: Xero is increasing prices to Accountants by charging developers for API access from 2026 onwards. Xero is leveraging access to our data, not Xero's data, to charge developers who build tools that accountants use. Developers will have to pass this onto their clients, who likely already pay Xero for their expensive software.
use_case: Xero provides cloud accounting software to my tax practice clients for their businesses.

likes: After researching multiple options, Xero was the best solution to meet the specific needs of our non profit organization with multiple locations managing finances separately. The cloud based interface and simple entry options are a big win for our team who wear lots of other hats beyond financial management. All the bells and whistles are available if we need them, but our busy staff can use Xero to manage finances in a simple way that frees up more of their time for other aspects of their job.
dislikes: Haven't noticed any major drawbacks. You can't just pick up the phone and call someone, but they have been very responsive through the customer needs form and you can always request a phone call if you prefer to talk with someone and they will call you.
use_case: We needed a way for all our locations to manage their separate finances from a cloud based platform that our main office could then access. Previously, all our offices used different systems and if an employee left, the access often went with them. Now we can control access and give access to new employees without losing data.

bonus_data.title: Review about the bookkeeping software Xero

likes: We appreciate the affordability in comparison with Quickbooks, without the loss of robust reporting. We also appreciate Xero's virtually seamless integrations with our other business applications, such as Gusto Payroll and ServiceM8.
dislikes: Xero's support process could stand some improvement, in regards to accessibility and addressing case details.
use_case: Xero's capability to create tracking categories saves us tremendous time when it comes to compiling the necessary financial data for the multiple municipal license renewals we have to complete. Additionally, with our ServiceM8 integration, I can push the work orders created during customer encounters straight to Xero for invoicing the customer. This saves me time having to rebuild invoice information to invoice from Xero. Xero makes my bank and credit card reconciliations a breeze with direct bank feeds.

switched_from.reason: We switched from Wave Financial (waveapps.com) because it did not offer the capability to breakdown work by service area. We needed this feature as our business expanded in order to accurately complete requirements for business license renewals over multiple municipalities. Xero is a more robust and technical system that meets our growing needs.

bonus_data.title: Xero accounting software for small businesses

likes: It is a very easy program to use, and I like the format of reporting etc (except new GST return - I prefer the old format). Support has been generally good.
dislikes:
I am starting to feel frustrated with the large price increases - it is becoming more difficult to 'sell' xero now as it is starting to become unaffordable for small business and not for profits. I would certainly appreciate a greater discount for registered charities - a number of payroll software providers offer their software for free but even if xero could offer 50% to charities that would be amazing.
Another thing that I would like to see changed is the charities reporting templates in the advisor section be available to all advisor users not just staff. Many tier 3&4 charities do their own reporting now but they cannot access the xero templates unless they are a staff member of a xero partner practice. This would help many many charities around NZ complete their accounts more efficiently.
use_case: It is definitely making bookkeeping easier for clients to do themselves, and making it easier for us to correct their errors.

likes: Xero is a really easy to use product and does not requires one to be a financial expertise to be able to use it. The interface is clean and friendly therefore surfing through it is always easy and effortless. Xero is also very reach in accounting features which simplify our work since we get everything we require to manage our finances from it without having to use any other products. The most incredible feature has been the financial reporting which helps us get insightful and detailed reports about our financial activities. The automations available make our work easier for example when entering data for invoicing. It is so easy to access Xero and it has very powerful security features which gives our financial data top security. Xero supports third party integrations and we were able to seamlessly integrate it with our project management products for budgeting and controlling project expenses. Xero also has analytical tools that help us analyze all our financial activities in order to understand our financial health track records.
dislikes: All the features in Xero are so user-friendly to use and they all deliver perfectly to our needs. Everything has been very okay such that there has been no downsides with Xero.
use_case: We are using Xero in our organization as an accounting tool and it has helped us incredibly to streamline our financial management practices. It helps us to gain visibility onto our business financial health track by analyzing our financial activities on how money is coming ang going out of the business. Xero also help us to do budgeting for our construction projects and also to track our projects expenditure in order to ensure we do not have unnecessary expenses. Xero gives us easy time when billing and creating invoices to our clients as well as purchase orders to the construction materials we purchase. Xero is really good for accounting.

likes: It's fairly well designed and easy-to-use software but it's not worth the very high price anymore
dislikes:
Last few years there have been price rises every year, at least 5-10%. Some years there have been two! Meanwhile, very little actual product improvement happens. The user interface gets little refreshes but long-standing bugs are not fixed and long-standing feature requests are not implemented.

Now (from 1st July 2024), they have removed payroll completly from the starter plan, so you have to go on to a plan that costs (quite literally) more than double to retian it. Same story for the higher plans, the number of payroll users has halved so you have to go on to a much more expensive plan to get what you have before. They claim "most features get more for what they pay" but seems to me every teir has only had features removed, as well as a 10% increase,
use_case: I need accounting software and Xero is accounting software! Just unfortunately it's not good value anymore for what you get.

Xero streamlines workflows and boosts productivity by integrating with a variety of third-party apps and services, including payroll software, CRM software, and payment processors.

Numerous accounting processes, including bank reconciliations, invoicing, and spending tracking, are automated by Xero, which saves users time and lowers the possibility of mistakes.

It's important to remember that not all users will experience these drawbacks, and many companies still find Xero to be a really useful and efficient accounting solution in spite of these difficulties.
use_case: Xero produces tax reports, streamlines electronic filing, and automates tax computations to assist businesses in adhering to tax laws and regulations. Users may quickly generate reports that are customized to match their unique requirements, giving them valuable information about how their organization is performing and where they can make improvements.All things considered, Xero may help firms by enhancing the effectiveness, precision, cooperation, and compliance of financial management procedures by tackling these typical accounting issues.

likes: Easy to use software and relatively clean and straightforward UI.
dislikes:
There are many little important features that are present in competitor products but not in Xero. Look through the forums and you'll see nothing but countless disgruntled customers complaining about why certain features aren't present, and Xero has absolutely no plans to implement what their customers ask for.

They also constantly hike up their prices. They justify this by saying that they pledge to improve the platform but the same issues have been around for YEARS.

The reality is that they increase prices because they know that their loyal customers will not migrate to their competitors due to the immense time, money and work that will be required to perform the migration.

Their customer support is also rubbish.

Preparing end of year financial statements could be far more streamlined: dates in the compilation report don't update automatically, page breaks could be easier/quicker to change, having to 'save draft' everytime a change is made seems unncessary and time consuming, having to go into 'edit layout' to make changes also seems unnessesary and time consuming.
Also: depreciation schedule is very inflexible, fixed assets should be able to have part sale, or part additions options.

likes: Xero for me is the easiest and the most user-friendly accounting software out there. It allows you to create bills, and invoices, and integrate your bank with Xero so you can directly reconcile every transaction in Xero, it can be integrated with other receipt bank software as well, this way you don't need to manually create those bills into Xero. I love how they made it so user can have the option to study and learn everything about Xero through their Learning feature. I have been using Xero every day for the past 2 years and it never let me down. Even though there are times that the system is down, you can easily check and search Xero's status online to see what's happening and what steps they are taking to fix the problem. It also allows you to create tickets when there are issues arising and you need proper support. The community also serves as customer support as most frequently asked questions have been answered. The implementation of product updates has been made easy as they make sure that customers are well-informed ahead of time and provide guides so they can manage everything as well.
dislikes: I do love the reports and how easily they can be understood, but I felt like it has wide room for improvement. Just like how I sometimes need a wider range of reports but what you can generate is only limited to what they have in Xero. It would be amazing if I could go to reports, for example, payroll reports, and then have the dropdown menu with all the pieces of information that were inputted or created in Xero. Furthermore, I felt like payroll can be improved as well, and having all the awards installed would really help business owners as well, as sometimes it is very difficult to keep up with all the changes going on within the ATO and state governments. But all in all, Xero is still the go-to when it comes to accounting software.
use_case: Xero is solving all the manual things going on within the accounting books. It also helps to ease day-to-day transactions like creating bills and invoices, especially bank reconciliation. The reports help you check and manage expenses and sales every day. USER

Additionally, while the platform offers robust features, some users may find certain advanced functionalities to be lacking compared to other accounting software options.

likes: Xero is very user friendly compared to other accounting software especially for small business owners who feel overwhelmed with the amount of compliance. Xero makes it very easy to convert files from other accounting software and assist with the entire process. Customer support is very efficient compared with other accounting software and provide relevant and useful answers together with follow up. Integration with third party apps is seamless with additional features and updates added regularly. Access is very stable and offer different methods of accessing the program.
dislikes: The downside of Xero is being able to access different Xero files at the same time for example businesses that have different company files and require to be open simultaneously. It's difficult to have different web browsers open in order to access the different files. The time out of being logged in could be extended to an option of a full 12 hours throughout the day especially when reconciling accounts, it's quite annoying to have to log back in several times throughout the day.

likes:
Xero allows my company to keep track fo expenses, revenue, and aging receivables in a timely and organized fashion. It's dashboards provide us with a bird's eye-view of the state of our company's finances. There is a relatively low learning curve, especially for users who aren't from finance/accounting backgrounds.
We have also utilized Xero's automation tools to send out invoices on a monthly basis and reduce man-effort in our finance department.
dislikes: Xero is limited in its customizability. For instance, there are only 2 tracking categories that can be added to each invoice. This is troublesome when we need to track our revenue in greater detail, e.g. product category, sales team, project ID.
use_case:
We use Xero to track our revenue, expenses, and aging receivables. We utilize the reference field on Xero invoices to tag each invoice to the relevant customer and contract. This allows us to break-down our monthly revenue by customer and contract.
With Xero's automation tools, we can also automate the sending of invoices and payment reminders which has helped us to reduce man-effort.

One major drawback is its inability to assist in batch payment file creation. Additionally, I have refrained from attempting to utilize it for international banking information, such as those containing Swift codes, as it seems incapable of accommodating such details. Another significant limitation is its failure to consolidate data from the two separate companies we have registered on the platform. Given these shortcomings, I am concerned that Xero may become obsolete for our requirements, as it lacks the essential features we currently need.

likes: We are a SME with 10 admin staff using it on a daily basis. Live bank feeds for daily reconciliation, uploading credit card expenses, attaching documents to relevant transactions are all very helpful for us. It would suit small business best I think where whoever is using the system - sales/bookkeeping/accounts payable need access to all aspects of the software - bank balances, reports, expenses. Payroll is simple, Account watch list on the dashboard is helpful to track any incorrectly coded transactions, Connects with most other software - ie inventory, payroll or job software pretty easily and seamlessly. Customer support is quick. Implentation was simple and easy
dislikes: We do our purchasing through Xero and we cannot attached PDF to purchase orders. And as there is 10 of us using it - staff either have hardly any access or all access (ie if you want access to accounts receivable reports for debt collection - you will also have access to bank balances). Accounts payable - you can only pay 200 bills in one ABA - which is not helpful for EOM payments as wehave to do EOM in 2 ABA batches. Cannot upload docs in payroll centre to emoloyee cards, such as doctors notes for sick leave.
use_case: helping us printing less paper! - all of our accounts payableis now paperless. We are also reconciling daily which is handy for cashflow

likes: What I like about Xero is its user-friendly interface. The dashboard is clean, designed intuitively and thus visually hence making it easy to get a snapshot of your financial situation at first glance. The software also makes robust offerings, including invoicing, expense tracking, and inventory management which can be helpful for small businesses hoping to simplify their operations forcefully.
dislikes:
A lot of the software showed up loaded with features but it proved to be quite a challenge to fully utilise it since I often wrested with functionality issues that impaired my ability to apply the software across my entire business.

Moreover, the cost of using Xero was one big pain point. The monthly bills were quite steep, especially considering my difficulties in fully leveraging the software's capabilities. This and my challenges in disseminating necessary information to my accountants and authorities made for a less-than-ideal experience.

In conclusion, while Xero had some merits and has the power to be a mighty tool for small businesses, it fell short of my expectations on several key elements. The steep learning point, high costs, and communication challenges made it fit less in my business needs.
use_case:
Though the road to Xero was rockier than I could have imagined, it's tough to deny that, in the end, the software addressed several critical issues for my little lobby consulting business.

First, when Xero brought a level of order to my financial management that was previously lacking, I was able to track income and expenses all in one place, along with the management of invoices and monitoring cash flow. This central approach to financial management saved me a tremendous amount of time and effort, allowing me the focus elsewhere on other aspects of my business.

Second, Xero's cloud-based system allowed me to control my finances from anywhere at any time. That's especially useful for my lobby consulting business, where I often find myself on the move, meeting with clients or attending various events. With Xero, I could easily access my financial data in the office or out and about.

Lastly, Xero's reporting capabilities were a significant asset. The software provided detailed financial reports that gave me a clear picture of my business's financial health. These insights were game changers in helping me make informed decisions about my strategy for business and planning for future scenarios.

In conclusion, while Xero posed specific issues I had to struggle with, it also offered solutions that streamlined my financial management, provided flexibility, and added valuable insight into my knowledge of my business's financial status. Although the benefits these positives overshadow were never fully realized because of the difficulties Xero presented for me but contributed positively towards my business operations.

When using Xero, not only are collections individualized based on the needs of the merchant, but it is quick and easy to create, send and track these collections. In addition, consistent invoices can be automatically stored and emailed from Xero. Xero has the ability to use categories and tracking tools so that Xero users can conveniently access their account database while viewing reports from areas or cost centers. Xero offers different support possibilities such as help desk, chat, knowledge and email support.

Xero also offers training possibilities through the web, videos, in-person classes, information boards and documentation.

bonus_data.title: My all in one financial machine

likes: At the outset, it seems incredibly helpful across invoicing and accounting.
dislikes:
The customer service is terrible. There are threads on their help pages with hundreds of customers complaining about terrible features – or functionality they're desperate to have, dating back years. Xero seems to do zero about it.

When you finally get through to them, they're unbelievably unhelpful. Their expenses plugin was expensive and a total farce – it was hard for my team to onboard, kept insisting that we needed to offer mileage, and also kept crashing to the extent that I couldn't invite my whole team. We killed it and switched to Zoho, which was effortless.

Small things are super irritating – I have to do a 2-factor auth pretty much every time I log in, even on the same day, on the same device, and after ticking a box that says, remember this browser. Why have the box if it doesn't work?

The reports are awkward, I have everything exported to Gsheets so we can organise them properly.

The multicurrency functionality is again expensive and doesn't work well. It's extremely difficult to run a single entity with clients in multiple countries.

And the problem is that it would be unbelievably painful to get off Xero because our whole sales history – and forward bookings – are already in there.

It's a trap.
use_case: It's great for invoicing. It was great when we were a smaller business without clients overseas paying in foreign currency. I thought it would be better to run payroll and expenses through the one platform but that was a disaster. We're now just back to invoicing and accounts, and manually working around all the problems Xero creates.

likes: Xero is better than Quickbooks for the small business owner without a lot of bookkeeping or accounting experience. I've found that Quickbooks requires that the user have a basic understanding of double entry bookkeeping to truly be successful. Xero does not.

use_case: Xero streamlines the bookkeeping and accounting processes from invoicing to receipt storage. With just a little time regularly, a business owner can have data at his or her finger tips for business decision making and ready for tax time to boot!

likes:
In my opinion, one of the best features of Xero is its intuitive user interface. The software is easy to navigate, and it makes it simple to manage all of your financial transactions in one place.

Another great feature of Xero is its ability to integrate with a wide range of third-party applications. This allows you to connect Xero to other tools you use in your business, such as your CRM, inventory management, and e-commerce platforms. This makes it easy to manage all aspects of your business from one central location.

One of the most valuable features of Xero for me is its real-time reporting capabilities. Xero allows you to track your financial performance in real-time, which means you can make informed business decisions quickly and easily. The software also offers a wide range of customizable reports, so you can get the information you need in a format that is easy to understand.

Another standout feature of Xero is its strong security measures. The software uses multiple layers of security to protect your financial data, and it is regularly audited to ensure that it is always up-to-date with the latest security standards.

Overall, Xero is an excellent choice for businesses looking to streamline their financial processes and make better-informed decisions. Its user-friendly interface, integration capabilities, real-time reporting, and strong security measures make it a valuable tool for managing your business finances.
dislikes:
As a user of Xero, I have found that the software has some limitations. One limitation is that it is only available as a cloud-based solution, which means that I need an internet connection in order to access it. This can be inconvenient for businesses that operate in remote locations or have unreliable internet access.

Another limitation is that Xero does not offer some of the advanced features that are available in other accounting software. For example, it does not have built-in support for inventory management or job costing, which may be important for my business.

In addition, Xero may not be the best option for businesses with complex financial needs. While the software is user-friendly and easy to use, it may not have the sophisticated features that I need.

Finally, Xero is not free. While it offers a range of pricing plans, I will need to pay to use the service. This may not be suitable for businesses with limited budgets.

Overall, while Xero has been useful for my business, it may not be suitable for everyone. Its cloud-based nature, limited advanced features, and cost may be limiting for some businesses.
use_case:
As a user of Xero, I have found that the software solves many of the problems associated with managing business finances. One problem that Xero solves is the need for a user-friendly interface. The software is easy to navigate, which makes it simple for me to manage all of my financial transactions in one place.

Another problem that Xero solves is the need to integrate with other business tools. The software allows me to connect it to other tools I use, such as my CRM, inventory management, and e-commerce platforms. This makes it easy for me to manage all aspects of my business from one central location.

One of the most valuable ways that Xero solves problems for me is its real-time reporting capabilities. Xero allows me to track my financial performance in real-time, which means I can make informed business decisions quickly and easily. The software also offers a wide range of customizable reports, so I can get the information I need in a format that is easy to understand.

Finally, Xero also solves the problem of security. The software uses multiple layers of security to protect my financial data, and it is regularly audited to ensure that it is always up-to-date with the latest security standards.

Overall, Xero has been beneficial for me by solving many of the problems associated with managing business finances. Its user-friendly interface, integration capabilities, real-time reporting, and strong security measures make it a valuable tool for managing my business finances. likes:
It does everything you need an accountancy program to do and more, it's easy to use as you start automating processes, so reconciliation eventually becomes a few clicks.
They are also at the right price point, as you only pay for what you need, you can add on extras, but you aren't paying for what you don't need, I do think that the pricing is just at the right point, if they increased the charges any further and we may feel differently.
They also listen to feedback with one exception (the need to see a stock line with no stock, we have some products made to order, and this is sadly not an issue they are going to fix), but in every respect, they do act on feedback, they have their group on C Space designed to test new ideas and fix issues, and it's a great community.
dislikes:
The inability to sell stock lines with zero stock means you have to find your own workaround.
The business reporting tools can sometimes need a lot of amending and aren't the easiest and quickest tools to find.

bonus_data.title: Xero is a great accounting solution for your small business

likes: We've used Xero for several years, and overall I would say it's a solid solution for any small business. It's relatively easy to use, cloud-based, and you can automate many aspects of bank transactions like reconciliation.
dislikes:
While Xero is really good in many ways, there are some really basic things that it lacks, which is annoying. For example, if you create a batch payment (pay multiple bills at once), it will create a batch file, which you should be able to upload to your bank, and it will automatically create and pay the transactions. But the batch file they generate is not customizable, and therefore it does not work with many banks. Our company uses Chase Bank, which is a very large organization, and it does not meet its format requirements. Considering how many banks exist, I would think that customizing the file output would have been implemented long ago.

Another example of basic functionality that is missing is bulk voiding or deleting of bill credits. For some reason, you cannot do this in bulk, which I find very odd.
use_case: Automating a lot of our day-to-day accounting and keeping our bank feeds in sync and reconciled. Affordable Small

likes: Xero is a simple to use, nondaunting accounting platform. I'm basing my review from the view of a small business owner, it works equally well for large corporations, I'm sure, but for the purpose of this review, let's keep it small. I run a small graphic design firm in Ireland and a simple solution to accounting was a must, as I am absolutely awful with anything to do with accounting. Xero offers a straightforward solution, ties in well with National and international bank accounts to allow for automatic reconciliation, which is a huge time saver. I have been using it since 2010 (it's now 2021) and I can firmly say for a small business, it's excellent value.
dislikes: Occasionally the bank balance shown will not align with the actual amount of money in the account. This may however be down to the fact that I work in multiple currencies.
use_case: It saves me heaps of time. I can invoice quickly and easily. I've linked it in with Stripe Payments which has improved the time I get paid by most clients significantly.

likes: It is easy to email quotes/invoices etc, and to the chain to turn quote to invoices and purchase orders. Also our accountant links directly in so they are happy too. Very easy GST returns including tracking adjustments since last return.
dislikes: The user roles are too inflexible. We have sales/inventory reports we would like staff to access, but we can't give them access without giving them accounts access, so we have to call off these reports for them. This is a major flaw and we'd love to see Xero change this. While we recognise it's an accounts package firs, not an inventory tool, it feels a natural request to have more individualised user role functions to allow for this type of thing. This is further exacerbated by the fact that Sales staff can only see the last 30 transactions for an inventory item. If they could change to see further back this would also be very helpful as wouldn't need to access the inventory reports as often for this info.
use_case: Remote access from anywhere we happen to be, and time saved moving from quote to invoice to purchase order and supplier invoice by carrying all data across from the first event.

likes: Xero is a very useful accounting package for small businesses to manage their day to day financial transactions. It is a very intuitive product which does not require extensive accounting knowledge. The product is very reasonably priced and is very good value for money. Overall I would recomment Xero to any small businesses over the other products available.
dislikes: As Xero is a simple program it is not useful for any transactions slightly more complex than normal. It is also somewhat difficult to build custom reports however the standard reports are useful nonetheless. Generally we export our Xero reports into Excel to present for our Board reports etc.
recommendations: Simplistic financial reporting that all relevant parties can understand. Our staff who are not financially focussed can use the platform for inputting purchase orders and invoices which saves a lot of time for the finance team.
use_case: If you are a small business looking for a simple, cost-effective and solid accounting package - Xero is perfect. If your business is a little bit more complex, or you intend to move to IFRS accounting or you are multi-national, then I would suggest Xero is too simplistic and another accounting package should be used.

use_case:
If you are a business owner and is looking for a software solution for your accounting needs, look no further than Xero.

Xero provides business owners with the ability to digitally issue invoice and also collect payment via PayPal and credit card. 

In addition, it has the reminded feature that will send a remind to customer who missed out on the payment.

When you are about to do your annual accounts, all you need is to invite your account into Xero and they will take over from there.

No more spending the time and effort to send bulk files to your bookkeeping or accountant anymore because everything is already recorded in Xero.

likes: The dashboard explains everything you would want to know, and if there are features you always use, you can tag them to show up in the dashboard. The automatic billing feature is also awesome. Takes me less than 5 minutes to set up a new client and start the auto-billing feature for future invoices.
dislikes: When linking to PayPal it only allows one PayPal account. I have 2vPayPal capturing accounts but I can only link one. It doesn't recognize that there are 2 different accounts.
use_case: It allows me to do all of my business bookkeeping and I can access everything via an app for my Android or Apple iPhone

likes: This is one of the best software for accounting. Easy to use and navigate. Reasonable price. Easy to create user and grant access to them as per the work they are handling. Xero program is user friendly and free with many invoices features which always produces efficient results. What I like most about Xero is that it is a cloud-based software, it is very easy to use.
dislikes: There is no such thing about Xero which I don't like.
recommendations: All accounting transaction can be handle and manage with Xero Software which is easy to use and user friendly. Xero to be the simplest and most cost effective solution to our accounting needs. Xero- A Perfect
use_case: I would highly recommend Xero as it's an excellent cloud-based accounting tool, very easy to set up and use.

likes: We liked that Xero was not based in any one operating system and offered portability. It seemed easier to use than our older clunkier systems, like Excel spreadsheets, or older versions of Quickbooks.
dislikes: Porting over data from our spreadsheet or from Clio was a nightmare and would never reconcile. Getting help at first was very problematic as it was in New Zealand. We stopped using it after about a year or two as it was making our accounting for our law firm more cumbersome, confusing and complex. They did try to help us resolve our integration issues but then we seemed to be caught in a situation where each softwares' support teams was telling us it was the other sides issue.
recommendations: Well none currently. As with any payroll or accounting function they are written for people coming from a current system, so what may seem intuitive for them is unusable or overly buggy to us.
use_case: If you are just starting Xero seems to be more helpful and is getting more integrated with Legal timekeeping like Clio. We did not use it on its own and I am not familiar with how it may be helpful in other industries. If you are a legacy or older user of some software it may not integrate fully or efficiently and you may be expected to manually reconcile years worth of data to get an accurate picture of your finances. They may have fixed this since we stopped using it, however.

bonus_data.title: xero manages and automates the company

likes:
Xero is one of the most popular buisiness based account solutions.

With a complex initial learning curve, this app allowed for online real time accounting and upto date paperless accounting. 

Such a time saver, a payroll service in an app.
dislikes:
The initial complexity is a bit of headache and unless for accounting trained you will struggle with the accounting terminology.

Some the fields can be super small at times, so be prepared to stare hard at the small symbols.
recommendations:
Effextly a payroll for my company, so instead of a team of accountants, a course later and a few hours a weeks you are your own accounting and wages department.

This app literally pays for itself.
use_case: Customer support needs work. Regular updates.

likes:
As far as cloud-based accounting software goes, Xero is one of the finest options. I am an accountant/bookkeeper so:

It's very user-friendly, designed to be usable for non-accountants. All the debit/credits etc. happen at backend and user does most things in plain language

Secondly, support. It's quite an old software with a large user-base so it's forums and Help (Xero Central) has articles on most of the common troubleshooting issues and guides for using features.

The productivity features that save time for accountants and power users like Global shortcuts, Find and Redo (mass transactions), automatic matching of transactions, labels etc.

It offers Xero advisor certification that helps regular users differentiate and showcase their expertise. and it is free, unlike say NetSuite.
dislikes:
The interface is friendly but it could use some modernization. 

Another thing as an accountant is that Xero didn't allow me to register as a partner because I didn't have existing clients on Xero. So I can't start over at Xero, unlike QuickBooks. I feel it a little unnecessarily elitist.
use_case: Small business accounting

bonus_data.title: Xero is the very feature rich, user-friendly and only software in same league as QuickBooks

likes: Xero was a huge upgrade going from Quickbooks desktop to an online system. It can be accessed from anywhere at anytime without having to deal with servers and firewalls. The interface is very user friendly and navigation is pretty straightforward and easy.
dislikes: There are a few things that I would like to receive notifications for that at the moment cannot be accomodated. For example, when an invoice is paid, there is currently no way to get an email notification/confirmation. I'm not sure if it's our internet connection, but sometimes loading an account can be a bit slow. I use it almost everywhere, home, work, travel, and the load times always are about the same no matter what the speed of the internet connection. Regardless, it's significantly faster than anytime I was using Quickbooks.
recommendations: The problems we have solved with Xero are the ability to receive payments online quickly, send and track invoices repeatedly with minimal effort, see an accounts historical events, and produce useful reports as needed. Generating quotes, invoices, purchase orders, bills, etc. is very easy. The repeating invoice function has also been tremendously helpful to us in ensuring that we are invoicing clients as needed.
use_case: Xero is 100% worth a look if you are searching for online accounting systems. The interface may not be for everyone, but for me it's really easy to use.

recommendations: The fact I can integrate this for my financial team is excellent. It's also HMRC compliant meaning money is saved by simply sending accounts straight over from Xero.
use_case: At the moment, I'd suggest waiting to see how the current crisis affects them. They appear to have been overwhelmed by what is happening so after sales support is virtually non-existent. My advice would be to take a free 30 day trial, so how they fare (the middle of a crisis is the best time to find out, for free, how accounting software really does work under pressure!) and then go from there.

With the automatic bank details, the time we need to reconcile the accounts for each month is minimal. use_case: I highly recommend Xero as it is an excellent cloud-based accounting tool, very easy to set up and use, so you can configure some items. bonus_data.title: Xero is superb and excellent business accounting platform

likes: Xero has finally surpassed QuickBooks Online as the best solution for small business accounting. The banking/payroll integrations work well, and the manual journal system is far superior to QBO. The fixed asset registry also beats QuickBooks Online in terms of functionality. Finally, Xero seems to be more reliable -- in the past 6 months of using Xero we have not experienced any downtime, whereas QBO seemed to be unavailable for some period of time at least every month or so.
dislikes: The report builder, while powerful in some ways, is difficult to use when it comes to multiple time periods (e.g. YTD reports). In addition there is no option to show account numbers on reports, which makes further manipulation more difficult (e.g. in Excel).
recommendations: Xero has everything we need from an accounting solution.
use_case: I was initially put off by the lack of support for sub-accounts, but after diving in deeper this turned out not to matter. The report builder allows grouping of accounts, which accomplishes the same thing, and actually helps streamline the chart of accounts since there is no need for "dummy" accounts to use as top-level aggregate accounts.

switched_from.reason: Needed an online solution for simultaneous multi-user access and automated backups, along with integrations with other systems such as payroll and banking. Xero is vastly superior to QB Desktop, and -- after lagging behind QuickBooks Online for a long time, is now the superior system for small business accounting and financial reporting.

What I like best about Xero is the ease of use. There's a dedicated community that you can turn to to answer any questions you have. I also really enjoy their app store, which provide a lot of integrations to improve the functionality of Xero. Everything from accounts payable to expense reports to revenue recognition. What I dislike about Xero is their budgeting tool. We need to do a lot of departmental budgeting. Xero right now doesn't have a tool that allows you to have budgets by department and then have them roll up into a master budget. If you're considering Xero, I'd recommend that you check out their app store. Check out the top rated apps for the functionality that you're looking for and speak to their sales representatives and how they integrate with Xero. bonus_data.title: The accounting software with greater flexibility of the market

likes:
When you configure certain elements, they remain registered in the system once they are valid, that is, when you want to use them again when they are registered, you remember them automatically, this saves a lot of time in the data inserts in the system.

The banking rules can also be configured and their coding is practically automatic, since you only have to perform a validation. This is really useful to configure and register different invoices.

The audit and security controls help you to review possible errors in the accounting section. Both errors that may have been committed by users and possible failures when inserting or modifying any specific data by the company. This audit prevents failures.
dislikes:
The contact database is somewhat difficult to use, since it is difficult to import or export easily. It could simplify the process of inserting contacts to make it easier.

When you have a client that has multiple projects with you it is more difficult and laborious to configure the invoices within the system.
recommendations:
The invoices of our company are now in the system for analysis, for subsequent revisions and also to be informed of all the expenses we have. At the same time there are also customer invoices and we can observe and control the benefits achieved through the projects carried out.

It is a comprehensive accounting system that allows us to have greater control over our accounts and we can review globally all the financial sections.
use_case: The expenses and benefits of a company must be reviewed and controlled in a very specific way, it must be a detailed review with a very complete information, since the financial area of a company is really important for its proper functioning. Xero helps to execute those actions and to have more peace of mind, since it informs you about all the aspects of your invoices in a complete way.

bonus_data.title: Configure and automate the accounting area

likes: I like the bank reconciliation, as it is very user friendly for people less used to traditional bank reconciliation. Many of our clients have been able to use it effectively with little input from ourselves.
dislikes: There can sometimes be a few pages to click through for important sections. For example, "VAT Return" is an important section to get to, however it isn't initially clear how to find it.
use_case: Xero has made it very easy for clients to do the bookkeeping for their company and share it with us real time.

bonus_data.title: A good online platform for accountants and business owners

likes: Easy to use and found reporting easy to understand (also submitting VAT return is easy). Have now used Xero for a few years in current business, and also used previously with another business as found helpful. Some of my clients also love working with Xero.
dislikes: Some of the navigation did not seem that intuitive to me and when I did have a problem a year or so ago the answers I got were not that helpful.
use_case: Understanding finances on a month to month basis and being able to understand likely VAT liability and then submit return automatically.

bonus_data.title: Easy to use and great for understanding your company finances

likes: It is quite easy to get the hang of Xero with its intuitive set up and ease of keeping track of all the main business accounts. Also easy to customise certain areas such as the P&L layout which is useful for internal reporting
dislikes: Can be hard to reconcile certain items especially if integrated with a US bank account and also difficult to reconcile manual entries.
recommendations: Was able to perform daily reconciliations and reassessments of the company’s financial position. Also enabled us to produce reporting for investors in certain formats
use_case: Great product to use but there are so tweaks that would make it easier to use.

likes: We like how easy it is to handle invoices and payments on the Xero platform. It allows to properly organize each payment, depending on the cost of work. Your tax management system is excellent, very broad. It has great Payroll capabilities, such as being able to make direct deposits or benefits management.
dislikes: We do not like that as inventory management software does not have mobile access, or retal inventory management. Its management of fixed assets is quite limited, as is its application of sales forecasts, which is why it is not used to conduct market research.
recommendations: Xero has solved all our fraud detection problems, and our creation of analytics reports is now much more optimized. It took us less to carry the metrics of each inventory. In addition, your tracking of expenses has helped us a lot to take better control of our ROI.
use_case: We highly recommend the use of Xero. It is an excellent platform to take complete control of everything that enters and leaves our business. It is perfect for handling purchase orders, and for carrying ACH payment processing. It also has a lot of capacity for databases.

use_case: Consider invoicing out of other platforms rather than Xero. We inoice using our homegrown subscription/billing system and bill customer's credit cards using Stripe. bonus_data.title: The best software out there for accountants by far!

likes: <v ->My name is Steven.</v> I am an accountant in the professional services field, and I give Xero a nine on 10. Xero is an incredible cloud accounting software that we use to deliver a cloud experience to our clients, that helps them put all their GLs, all of their accounts all in one place, accessible on all devices. The best part about Xero is definitely the add-ons that we have, that you could plug into so many apps with their API. It gives us so much flexibility, and it definitely makes for endless opportunities. One thing that could definitely be improved in Xero is definitely the bank feed. There are oftentimes multiple transactions that are pulled on a duplicate basis, which makes it a bit difficult to trust the automated scripts. But other than that, it's a really great software. Definitely I would recommend that if you're considering using Xero, the best thing to do is just try, try. They have a very generous free trial, and you could call them up, and they'll help you out with your business, whatever your business needs specifically.

likes: Accounting was probably the most intimidating part of starting a business for the first time. I went with Xero because it looked less intimidating than QBO at the time and it supported payroll in my state for a very good rate. $29 for accounting with payroll up to 5 employees. Of all the features, payroll felt the most well thought out and complete.
dislikes:
As business grew I noticed most features felt unpolished and were lacking functionality that would make them feel complete. To add the functionality that you expect would have been built in required several apps each with their own monthly fee. 

Some examples
- You can see a list of your recurring invoices but you can't get a total unless you use a calculator or sign up for a separate monthly service.
- late payment reminders don't go to the invoice recipient. They go to the main contact. This caused a huge problem with our customers as we assumed invoice reminders were going to the right people. 
- can convert quote to invoice but not to repeating invoice.
- can't show passed due amount in current invoice
- navigation doesn't always take you to the right screen after you complete an action
- Why are my credit cards accounts listed when selecting paid to on invoices?
- support is abysmal, send an email and wait a couple of days for response.
- easy to migrate to xero as you can import a quickbooks file but no such

There's more but you get the point.
use_case:
Xero recently sent out an email indicating they are eliminating payroll and we must start using Gusto or another service. In addition the $29 product sees no reduction in price or added features which effectively triples the cost. That now makes them more expensive than QBO and not even close to being as robust. So this will be our last year with xero as we move to QBO.

Suggestion if someone from xero leadership reads this. Maybe stop throwing parties all over the world and focus on the product a little more. Was great when I first started but now I'm realizing

bonus_data.title: Xero accounts on my mobile Xero

likes: Overall, Xero is a great Accounting package. But, their recent and very shifty and deceptive decision to remove core features and start offering them as add-ons is disgusting.
dislikes:
Until recently, there wasn't much I didn't like. I probably couldn't name anything, apart from the somewhat dated UI - but that's a stretch.

Recently however, Xero have taken a core feature (Expenses) and starting selling it as an add-on, on top of the monthly subscription fee.

Expenses as you may know, and would expect, is 'included' in the monthly subscription and formed part of the core software. There was no additional charge. They've recently updated the Expenses feature (which they should be doing anyway) and now believe that because they've updated it, that warrants it being pulled from core and sold separately as an add-on.

As a current subscriber, I signed up for the all inclusive package. Having features ripped from the core package and charged for in addtion to the monthly subscription is not on. Especially when no discount or reduction of the monthly subscription cost was offered.

As a SaaS provider, any and all future updates, regardless of how big or small they are, should form part of the monthly subscription.

Choosing an accounting package is a big decision and once you're committed, it's difficult and often costly to change. With Xero now employing this kind of model, I'd caution anyone considering Xero to think again, think hard.

Today it's Expenses, tomorrow it could be something else.

These disgusting tactics employed by Xero are unacceptable.
recommendations: Basic accounting. Disgusting tactics now being employed. We aware.
use_case: With their recent and very shifty move to start removing core features and start charging for them as add-ons in addition to the full monthly subscription costs, I'd very carefully consider Xero. At any time they are likely to remove other core features and start charging them. It could be a costly exercise.

One other thing I dislike about Xero, which is probably because it has spoiled me in the first place, is the frequent disconnects through Yodlee when trying to synchronize banks.

use_case: My first recommendation would be to contact a local representative to do a live demonstration of Xero. Follow up this first meeting with a local Xero function - this may be a Xero Hour monthly meeting or a Roadshow in your area. Finally, talk with any number of accountant-advisors who utilize Xero for their accounting solution. From there, make an informed decision to join the Xero family. Finally, if you do choose to use Xero, take advantage of the numerous resources for training and branding Xero has to offer.

likes: It's comprehensive, no doubt about that. I know many people running their businesses on Xero and as far as an online accounting platform goes, it does the job
dislikes:
Primarily, their lack of implementing user-requested features. If you head on over to their user community forum you will see hundreds of requests, with hundreds of comments voicing support, for various features which have gone ignored for YEARS. 

Recently, Xero took to removing the dates from the user forum, no doubt because it looked bad having requests from 2005 be constantly bumped to the top of the forum evert week. Always the comments are the same: "Hey, I came here with the same issue! We would like to be able to change the default naming of invoice PDFs that are sent via Xero. Cant believe this was first requested in 2005!"

This is the same for a large number of seemingly simple features, including PDF naming, invoice email templates and invoice line-item notations.
recommendations: Simple. Easy bookkeeping and reporting, invoicing etc. Tired
use_case: If you have a high standard for how your invoicing and brand looks when using Xero to interact with customers via invoicing etc, then you are probably better getting dedicated invoicing software. If you are a small business, there are better cheaper options available. And if you expect to be able to make feature requests for software that you are paying for, then you will want to find another product.

bonus_data.title: UI and a lack of implimenting user-requested features

likes: Xero could be accessed anywhere as long as there is an internet connection since it is a cloud-based application. It has a nice modern user-interface that is quite user-friendly. They have updated the names and terminology of modules for non-accountants to use. For instance Accounts Payable is called Purchases and Accounts Receivable is called Sales.
dislikes: Xero does not have the ability for printing Canadian cheques. The cheque printing templates available are only for the United States and for some countries in Europe. The available reports within Xero are very limited as well. I continue to use a legacy report that captures most of the data that I need, but I have been told by Xero Support that eventually that report will be phased out. Xero had included a free expense claims module for the past few years, but that will be phased out at the end of September to a per user pay module just to enter expense claims.
recommendations: We wanted an accounting system that was easily accessible for the executives while they were away on vacation. Since it is a cloud-based application, Xero could be accessed anywhere in the world that has an internet connection. Another problem that Xero solved was that staff were able to log on and submit their own expense claims, instead of having the accounting department look after the input of receipts for the staff expense claims. Cloud-Based
use_case: Xero does not have the greatest tech support available. I was only able to talk on the phone with Xero tech support once since I kept on escalating the issue on reports to them. At the end, they couldn't provide me with a solution. Xero support suggested that I write on the blog for the Xero Community to see if other companies are encountering the same issues.

switched_from.reason: Simply Accounting did not allow for multi-user submission of expense claims. Per user licenses had to be purchased in order for staff to be able to submit their own expense claims.

bonus_data.title: The best accounting software for SME's

likes: We like that it's very easy to use and, as a start-up company, we can create and manage invoices with clients in a professional way. The recurring invoices functionality and tracking features are particularly useful for us. We also use Xero for employees to submit and manage their expenses and are currently exploring their holiday management tool.
dislikes: The reporting functionalities could be improved to provide greater visibility over what's been paid, what's been submitted and what's overdue. The existing features only provide a confused version of this information. We have multiple admin users but Xero offers no way of assigning invoices to specific users.
recommendations: Raising invoices is a necessity in our business however, as a start-up business, we do not have an accountant who can manage this for us. Xero offers a seamless and cost-efficient way of carrying out this work: it couldn't be simpler and cheaper.
use_case: Make sure you know how you'll manage responsibilities within the software

likes: I chose Xero because it’s core functionality is financial and management accounting. Critical features for small business owners, midsize business, and non-profit members are in the application. As an accountant, it’s amazing to work on the “advisor side” of the application. It’s a cloud application that’s robust, has outstanding uptime, works across devices, and presents a financial picture with ease. The reason I say core application, is because it so easily integrates with 100’s of applications. That’s important, because I didn’t want application that selects for example a CRM or inventory tracking system for me. They make sure they’re the best at what they do and then the user can select the best tool for them to seamlessly integrate.
dislikes: I don’t like that as you continue to reconcile throughout the year, prior reports no longer show items that were open during that period. When going through a financial statement audit, it’s important. We publish the reports now, so they’re saved in the system and there’s an audit trail. However, I feel like this could be improved.
recommendations: We’re an accounting firm and we keep our books on Xero and our clients use Xero. It means we not only support their accounting but we can help them with technical issues real time. We don’t use a lot of different accounting applications, one because we love Xero, and two because, this way we can fully support the business-owner. Often, for a business owner, or Executive Director, questions relating to accounting can be technical matters in the application. Between, Xero certification, and killer customer service/technical support, we’re able to provide the advice our clients need.
use_case: Take advantage of the advisor directory to ask questions. I think most of us are happy to answer. If not, watch videos and use a trial. If you’re converting from an application, be sure your books are clean. I’d get in there and play with the tools first. While both QBO and Xero are cloud applications, their approaches are very different. The terms Xero use are really business-owner or non-profit friend and I think you’ll find working in there, it’s easy to work through and keep up with your books. Also, look at add-ones to get an idea of what might make sense to you. There are many you really don’t need right away, so Xero is your starting place, but occasionally your business needs will result in adding an application. You can really brand and get invoices just the way that works for you. But, it’s worth taking it step-by-step and really learning the features.

bonus_data.title: Strong financial accounting platform with great add-ons and management reporting

switched_from.reason: While there were plenty of great features in QBO, it also has too many items built-in that didn’t appeal to me. I don’t like the usability of it or process of accounting. I think it’s got to many areas where a small business owners can go wrong. I would say it’s a strong accounting tool, but just not as clean as Xero and not as easily integrated with other applications.

recommendations: Easy-to-use finance software. For benefits, we have multiple people around the world using it, from the accountants in the US to the CEO of our parent company in Europe.
use_case: If you are already familiar with accounting software, and you are looking for sophistication, Xero is not for you. Xero is a great entry-level finance package.

You don’t need to spend long looking for anything - it just seems arranged in order of the way that you’d actually need it. Appearance-wise, it’s also very clean, which contributes to how simple it is to use. I also like the mobile app, as it's really easy to use and makes doing expenses very simple.
dislikes: It’s hard to identify anything specifically as I’m actually really pleased with my experience on it. I only use it for the occasional expense and to check my payslips. It could perhaps benefit from email notifications when expense claims are approved or payslips come in.
