reviewer_name: Verified User in Information Technology and Services

likes:
Xero is a full-blown accounting software perfect for small- to medium-sized business. It's easy to use, works reliably and offers an extended API that allows you to integrate it with your existing systems.
What we like about it:
-Easy to use: The interface is very easy to navigate and self-explanatory. They also offer detailed documentation and have a great support forum.
-API: This the most valuable asset for us. It allows to integrate it with our internal systems and to almost fully automate many repetitive accounting tasks.
-integration with your merchant/payment accounts: Let's your customers pay you online easily.
dislikes:
Performance could be better, sometimes we are experiencing noticeable slow-downs. Also, but that's the nature of the offering, one might have concerns about having all their sensitive business data in the cloud. So I'd highly recommend keeping local backups of all data.
Another drawback: The import feeds (to connect your bank accounts) sometimes don't work.
recommendations: It allows to fully run our accounting and payroll in the cloud and doing it in-house versus outsourcing it to a pricey provider. Great accounting software, sophisticated
use_case: They make it very easy to switch fro Quickbooks, don't let that scare you off. Also consider the added costs when running Payroll with Quickbooks, which adds significantly to the total monthly price. We came to the conclusion that Xero offers a much better offering at a much more competitive price that QuickBooks.

bonus_data.title: API, easy to integrate, all at a fair price

likes: Xero is saving many hours of time each day compared to using Sage 50. I am very impressed. We moved from Sage 50 around 3 months ago and am starting the see the many benefits of cloud accounting, and the simplicity which Xero does things.
dislikes: The move from sage to Xero could have been a little smoother, because we have multi currency a lot of things had to be fixed manually. We still have a small problem of some historic invoices being in a wrong currency. however because you can accept payment against any invoice in any currency (unlike Sage) this is less of an issue.
recommendations: Time is the biggest problem we have solved, and also being able to see live bad debtors and creditors is a huge positive. The bank feeds prevent me from having to log into every bank each morning, i simply hit refresh and it pulls in the latest incomings and outgoings of every account we have. This makes bank reconciliation a much easier and smoother process. I always had to fight with Sage at month end to do this, but now it is a doddle. New User but very impressed. Moved from
use_case: Spend a lot of time on the implementation and get it right from the offset.

switched_from.reason: Sage was getting very tedious and had to be ran on a server. We wanted cloud computing. The fact that Sage did not run on a mac was a big problem for us.

likes:
Hard to include everything but for the average small business it really is your one stop shop for your accounting requirements:

Customised reports
Pleasant UI
Ease of access (destop / mobile / no user restriction / invite your accountant)
Bank reconciliation is made super easy and can complete from your mobile phone also
Super easy invoicing (accept online payment, custom templates, auto invoice reminders)
Multi currency with Xe.com rates saves a heap of time compared to the old way, makes reconciliations easy and auto books the gains / losses for you. Really don't have to think too much about it.
Xero touch app (alerts on touch smartpone app for changes in daily bank balance also)
Ample integrated apps (CRM, Inventory, Document storage, Payroll etc)
Free xero education / learning platform
Wealth of support (help centre / support tickets)
Xero community
dislikes:
Currently does not support other languages except English (I do consulting to Japanese / Chinese based businesses which have come up with some nice work arounds).
Budget manager is for performance budgets only (not cash or balance sheet) but in saying that you have available integration apps that can do full budgets for you.
recommendations:
Access anytime anywhere.
Customise reports to suit business owner / stakeholder needs
Make recording source data (bank transactions, loose expenses) as painless as possible with little effort, by using automated bank feeds, xero touch app, xero expenses app.
It's proven to save larges amounts of time for the clients I've assisted and also allowed for visibility over business operations where previously there was a strong reliance on the accounting division before understanding where your business was at.
use_case: Before diving in, map out all your business applications, check that everything is going to play nicely together (API connections), if needed consider Zapier to connect external applications to Xero (can do some very clever things), importantly understand Xero is not like quickbooks, MYOB, Sage etc...the onboarding / setup process therefore is going to be different than what you are used to. Xero is suited for small business applications, if you are a large scale manufacturing firm or hold complex stock items don't expect Xero to do you any wonders. Check that it meets your requirements first. If stuck refer to a certified Xero adviser or Xero support for assistance who are quick to respond. Happy accounting.

bonus_data.title: Feature rich, simple to use, high level support and training documentation

likes: I find Xero intuitive. The concept of transaction reconciliation as laid out in Xero makes sense to me. Automatic transaction imports are very convenient and less error-prone.
dislikes: I don't like Xero's inventory functionality and I also wish Xero had more connections. I'd like to automatically import my Amazon, eBay, Walmart, etc. merchant transactions.
use_case: I was previously paying $140 monthly for bookkeeping service that did not meet my quality expectations. With Xero, I spend about an hour each month preparing the books and the software only costs me $30/month.

likes: The biggest this is that you don't need a accounting degree to use it. It sync with everything I currently use and it's quite cheap to use. You don't have to pay someone to set it up for you especially if you are willing to do some digging and find the answers to your questions.
dislikes: It can be a bit difficult to setup but once you are there it's easy to use. I could use a few more custom reports too.
use_case: We needed a solution that was easy to use and easy to teach others to use. It also had to be online and have lots of add-on's and apps to sync to. Xero fill all of those blanks. To start with we were a one-man-band so it had to be simple to setup and just run and without any form of accounting experience it was simple and easy. Simple

switched_from.reason: Wave just didn't have the functionally we needed.

likes: The ease of payroll and expenses, pay people with a click of a button. Employees capture images of receipts and input info about the expense on their own xero app, available on Android & iOS. You can then approve/decline & make payment with ease.
dislikes: There’s little to not like about xero, sometimes migration from another system is a challenge but more time times than often the transistion is smooth.
use_case: I am now an ablento work from home which is a great benefit, xero Ian completely cloud/web based meaning anywhere you can access the internet, you can work. Not a lot really trainign needed at all.

likes: Really easy to use and can import invoice information exported from POS system. Extremely customizable reports.
dislikes: Payroll has been discontinued as of December 2018.
recommendations: Affordable accounting software with advanced capabilities. Streamlined having POS sales put into the accounting system by using Xero's import functionality. Easy to Use &
use_case: Great alternative to Quickbooks and customer support is extremely responsive.

switched_from.reason: Xero is much more affordable and easier to use. Reports are more customizable.

likes: I love the open access to the Xero API. We are able to pull data in and out from all of our other programs. The user interface was put together with a lot of thought.
dislikes: I dislike the limited functionality that the Android app offers. Also, it would be great if there was a UDF for clients to choose the payment amount when viewing/submitting transactions from a invoice. Currently, the only option is to pay in full so we're unable to collect deposits or partial payments for projects.
recommendations: Clearly, accounting is the primary reason for Xero. I also enjoy the gateway options for receiving client payments. Tax tracking is also a great feature.
use_case: After almost 10 years using Quick Books, we made the switch. Best decision to date. Go

switched_from.reason: QBO did not meet our needs.

likes: Easy of use and drill down ability from P&L to an invoice. Integration with our Business Management Software (Filemaker) and Deputy
dislikes: Nothing at the moment, nothing at the moment
recommendations: Access from multiple locations. Flexible working Promising
use_case: We moved from Sage Accounts package. The best thing we have ever done.

likes: It's easy to use and it rarely breaks. Very cost effective and our accountant works with it so he's able to login to it to make sure all reconciliations and payroll are being done. We have as many users as needed connected simultaneously to the platform with no additional fee.
dislikes: We don't have an option for local accounting. So when the internet is out, you have no options of using the accounting system. However, this is true for Quickbooks Online as well so not much you can do there. There have only been 2 times when their own website was affected and we could not use it but it was fixed within 24 hours.
recommendations: It has great if you need to connect with other platforms such as Hubspot, PandaDoc, Etc. Research the integrations that are available and you'll see that it can make your life a lot easier.
use_case: Xero integrates with MANY different software and platforms so see if its a good fit for you. We like it, it works well for us but then again we are a marketing agency, so we do not manage any inventory. I would not be able to talk about that. We are forever grateful that we found Xero because at the time we were between this and QB Online which from what we have heard is not as good. After our experience with the support team at QB for the Desktop version we would never consider going back to them.
reviewer_name: Verified User in Marketing and Advertising

bonus_data.title: Best software for accounting in small businesses!

switched_from.reason: We did not like the support for the product. Out accounting file kept on getting "Corrupt" and we had to send them the entire compressed company file for them to fix. This did not seem logical. Their software was damaging the file, not us. At some point, the reconciliation with the bank was not working properly and they expected us to pay around $600 this made no sense.

likes:
Nearly everything. I love the fact that it's cloud-based, so accessible from anywhere. The small monthly cost includes unlimited users which is great. 
It integrates with other platforms which means we can create automation links easily.
It's pretty easy to set up and learn too.
dislikes:
Accruals & prepayments could be easier. 
One of our biggest issues is lack of granularity for user permissions. This is still a bit too clunky.
We would like a paid phone support option or a live chat support. Although support is prompt, it's still very stop/start by email
recommendations:
Available anywhere, cloud-based. 
Simple to use.
Integrates with other platforms with API
use_case: Get it, it's fantastic. If Xero can fix the very few small issues, it would be perfect.

bonus_data.title: Not perfect but brilliant accounts solution for small business

switched_from.reason: Sage wasn't cloud-based. Xero is on a totally different level. It's not fair to Xero to compare the two, Sage has been left behind

likes: Very easy navigation and simple to use. No complicated features. Also has a mobile app so you can easily upload expenses. There is also a feature where you can invite your accountant to manage the accounts for you and you can set invoice reminders at whatever internal you deem necessary. For example, you can send a reminder for payment the day before it is due, when its overdue etc. so you can keep on top of late payers.
dislikes: Reporting could be a little more graph based to make them more intuitive but apart from that, really good reports. It would also be good if you could further customise the dashboard and the cash in/out timeframe. Seeing this for the whole financial year would be good as well as having the ability to compare against your budget so you can see how you are performing against your forecasts.
recommendations: We use it to manage all of our finance from sales orders to purchases. We use this to reduce the time it takes to receive payments as Xero has an automated reminder you can set to remind clients 1 day before payment is due, after payment is late etc. You can also create multiple revenue streams allowing you to identify where most of your sales come from in terms of particular products, goods or services which of course filter through to the reports.
use_case: Take a look at their reporting elements.

likes: The API integration is super easy. I was able to set up my website (custom coded) to add transactions into Xero very quickly. I really like the blue color it's kinda nice and soothing. No fancy graphics just simple. The ability to customize an invoice to have different brands is nice. That way I don't need to change the logo, payment account etc, every time.
dislikes: The navigation sometimes takes a few more clicks. So if I want want to view all invoices it takes 3 clicks. Also the timeout is very quick. Sometimes it can be confusing as to how the "contacts" work as they aren't assigned a customer or vendor status until they are actually used and then they can flip back/worth. So that can make life a little bit confusing than before
recommendations: Accounting, It provides a much better solution than peachtree that I was using before. Peachtree was very old and it didn't integrate with anything. With XERO I was able to set it up to work with a special coding I already had in place. To create Invoices & Receipts based on live transactions. I have noticed a significant less amount of time doing entry since many of my sales are <$5.00 and to one time customers. Excellent Software. Good
use_case: I would definitely look into it! Great Software, and it works with a lot of other SAAS too!

switched_from.reason: Needed a more comprehensive solution That worked with my current payment processors, offered a good API, Payroll, invoicing and expense reporting. Freshbooks does good with invoicing but with the "new" freshbooks they don't support our payment processors rendering it useless.

likes: I really like that Xero is cloud based. Further, I love the dashboard, ease of reconciliations, and notes of communications with clients and vendors. I really like that I can see which clients have opened up my invoices for review.
dislikes: I don't like that the system won't allow for overpayments or for open credits to sit on accounts. Further, I find that often times PO's remain opened even after a final bill has been created which occasionally cases confusion.
use_case: I like that members of our team can get a quick snapshot of our financials at anytime, and that anyone can check in on the status of communications because there is a thread for every invoice, PO, bill etc. For our busy team this works perfectly for us.

bonus_data.title: Xero - a great solution for small business

likes: There are many things I like about Xero, all it's interfaces are very user friendly , my favourite difference from Sage is the bank reconciliation , from bank transactions automatically fed from the bank and being able to create, amend and adjust items on the same screen takes the work out of it. I use both accounts and payroll software and the user friendly interfaces allow you to complete your work with ease.
dislikes: To be honest I haven't actually found something yet which I dislike , I have been a user now for 7 months and really love the software,
recommendations: The benefit it provides to my business is that I as a book-keeper and the client can both work at the same time , paperwork can be uploaded by the client and work can be completed off site . Reports and accounts can be produced with ease, and reports and easy to find and laid out in a professional manner. My
use_case: Make the change you will not look back , it's so much easier to use and find reports!

switched_from.reason: I chose to switch for the functions Xero had to offer compared to the Sage options, Sage 50 is like a dinosaur compared to Xero

dislikes: really steep learning curve takes a little while to grasp. Color schemes could use a little revamping as well as the interface as a whole

dislikes: There is not much I dislike about Xero. If I had a chance to make one thing better I would say the ability for Xero to pull in my bank account balance but I do not believe our inability to do that is something caused by Xero, our bank is just not easy to work with and requires us to manually update Xero.
recommendations: Measuring our spend and monitoring our available cash and credit line

bonus_data.title: Secure, simple, and fast accounting software

likes: Most small business owners are not super detail-oriented, expert accountants, and therefore can't, or won't use all but a small fraction of tools that most of the industry "standard" accounting suites offer. Thus, almost all accounting packages are super tough to use, and so go unused. Xero is different. It has only the tools you need and will actually use, and in an interface that allows you to get around quickly. I actually enjoy working with my accounting software now.
dislikes: There are a few integrations that it could use that would make it perfect. But they are not mission critical (different payment processors for receiving payment on invoices, for example.) . Also, I'd expect a company like Xero to be adding or improving features more frequently than they do.
use_case: We needed to be able to access our accounting software from multiple computers, within an interface that would allow us to get around quickly. By not being locked into a single machine (or multiple expensive licenses and clunky syncing systems!), we can access our books when and where we need to, cheaply and easily. Awesome software that

bonus_data.title: ACTUALLY lets you do your books quickly

switched_from.reason: Quickbooks is clunky, difficult to use, expensive, and locked into a single machine. Their online version is ill-conceived, missing features, and an afterthought. Xero is modern, cloud-based, user-tested, excellent software.

likes: We love Xero, it is absolutely perfect if you have basic accounting needs. It has all the simple accounting needed for small business and the ease of use makes the barrier to entry perfect fort the amount of time most people want to spend on getting to know the product.
dislikes: I have noticed that they tend to have poor customer service. They rarely reply to tickets and sometimes if they do reply it does not clear anything up. That being said, they have a great community which helps you find solutions. If it was a little cheaper, the package would be perfect.
recommendations: I can use it anywhere which is awesome - on my phone, tablet and computer. It is also so easy to use because you cane take a photo/scanning an invoice to attach to the payment. It also makes keeping payroll updated easy. The tracking in other currencies and being able to separate that out has been incredibly helpful for us as we operate internationally. Perfect for
use_case: Good functionality if you are simply creating invoices or tracking payables. Not good if you are tracking inventory on a regular basis. You do need to have some patience when using it and go online to find answers because as I mentioned earlier - the support from the company itself is not very helpful. But its great for people who need basic account and allows you to track in different currency's which is great if you are doing business in other places.

likes: Very easy to use and understand for the non-accountant. My clients love this software because it uses simple terms, not accounting terms, like “spend money” and “receive money” instead of credit and debit. There are many integrations that help streamline the accounting process.

recommendations: It takes care of my accounting in the background, so I can spend more time on my business. The most basic benefit I'm seeing recently is now that it's all set up, it's crazy easy for me to see my basic numbers- money going out and money coming in.

dislikes: Limited functionality in budget manager.

use_case: One limitation is that there are only two dimensions that you can attach to an entry. Make sure you do not need more than that for the foreseeable future. In addition, Xero support is limited so be sure you have someone capable of figuring things out on their own. Finally, be aware that Xero has a done of great, seamless integrations so if Xero doesn't offer something you are looking for, be sure to check out their integrations as it is likely you can piece together a customized solution for you.

likes: The simple interface and flexible workflows make Xero really easy to use. It integrates with Stripe so it's easy to take online payments for invoices.
dislikes: Only difficulty we've had is with purchase order numbers - as far as I know these can only be added in the invoice description.

bonus_data.title: Really easy to use for a 'non accountant'

likes: The software automates the process of reconciling purchases based on previous entries, which can be helpful. Everything is available on mobile, And your accountant can login to see your activity in real time, which is cool.
dislikes: The software is anything but beautiful. Let’s be honest, Excel is prettier that their platform. Xero is boxy at best and doesn’t have the reporting functionality of something like Google Sheets. Best for small businesses.
recommendations: Doing basic accounting and tracking funds flow.
use_case: Think about what you really needs from your software first. And when you’re spending too much time trying to implement it, remember that sometimes it’s the software... not you.

likes:
Very clean interface for billing and sending invoices. When you see what the client is sent is it a very professional interaction. We can also set up click to pay for our clients to make bill pay easy. 
We are using the workflow feature for a multi-step review process before anything is released. That is a nice feature. 

I also like the Dashboards and ability to search and find saved invoices. The ability to copy and old invoice is also very helpful.
dislikes: Since I am not in the system all the time I find that sometimes I am not sure if an invoice is really sent to a client. I tend to make sure I am cc'd. We also have some issues with workflow where the approver may send a draft to a client because they can see an invoice created right away.
use_case: Invoicing and purchase orders.

likes: I am not a CFO or Accountant and yet I can find my way around and do most things that a small business owner will need to do.
dislikes: Support sometimes feels a little remote / too community based.
use_case: Simplified accounting, good links to other apps

likes: It's an online software so you don't need to download and install anything. Also you can access the account anywhere and any time as long as you have internet access. It's easy to share data with customers and accountants as everything is shown online.
dislikes: If the internet is slow, sometimes it's hard to open the website and it's hard to get to the website in some countries.
use_case: Accounting and management process can be solved using Xero. it's easy and fast to share up to date data with other people.

likes:
I like using Xero Accounting for the following reasons:
1. It's Easy to use, understand or grasp even though I'm not a CA.
2. Integration with almost any platform you can think of, seamlessly.
3. I like blue.
4. It's online, no backup issues, server crashing, tape backups, all my data is where it's supposed to be.
5. Templates for any documentation you can think of, even the emails sent out to your clients happens smoothly, whether they are sent manually, automated yearly, monthly, daily etc.
6. After 5 years, I'm still the happiest pig around.
dislikes: The mobile application can include more features, but since I'm a developer. I know that the mobile application space will eventually move back to the cloud anyway.
recommendations: My auditors phoned me up near the end of my company's first year in business. They needed my accounting history, whether it's receipts, little notepads lying around invoices made by hand etc. I browsed to www.xero.com and added my auditor to my company as an accountant with read-only access. Xero sent them an invitation to which they could create their own password and BANG. 15 minutes later my audit was done with the results requested. Sit back, Relax and let XERO run your
use_case: Nothing else matters. I logged one issue in more than 5 years, mobile/web based apps included. That is a track record you can be proud of.

likes: It's web based, looks good, and works (ish) with many systems through the API. They fixed the Stripe integration, finally, which was a problem for years.
dislikes:
First and foremost, if you need to use Purchase Orders, Xero isn't for you. You cannot include Item Codes (their version of Part Numbers) on purchase orders. You can use the Item Name, but even to get that you need to create a custom template and rework it entirely in Word to get that. The default setting from Xero is that you only get Item Description. This is obviously useless for any purchasing, and Xero has clearly stated that it is never going to happen because, and I quote, "Item Codes are mainly for internal purposes." It's a huge issue. https://community.xero.com/business/discussion/3361072 is the thread on their boards where they refuse to fix it.

In addition, accountants can't make certain kinds of journal entries which they're used to being able to do in Quickbooks. This causes an issue if you're making the switch, so make sure your bookkeeper or accountant is trained on Xero.
recommendations: General book keeping, flowing in sales from multiple channels, expenses. Started Out
use_case: I'd be wary if you're in a B2B situation. They're set up for B2C, but if you need normal functionality like Quotes and POs, you're in for some headaches.

bonus_data.title: Good, but too many details missed and processes screwy

As an online software company, it is usual to have bugs when a new version is released. However, when Xero has bugs it changes things that can cause issues in your accounting system i.e. changing a customers invoice to GST INCL instead of EXCL etc. 

If you make changes to a repeating invoice, journal etc the notes give a time and a date but not what was changed.

Help is only available through email which is fine most of the time but sometimes you do need someone to speak to.

likes: The Xero Interface is beautiful. The platform is awesome.

likes:
Allows me to view the financial position of my company at a glance (including converting invoices in foreign currencies) and plan cashflow which is vital to us as a new business.

Raising invoices is simple, we've integrated with Stripe (super easy to set up) to allow customers to pay invoices by card which helps us take payments easily and quickly. VAT returns have gone from being a bit of a headache to a 30 second task and the Payroll system is much easier to use than the HMRC tool.

Genuinely one of the best decisions we've made as a business to start using Xero.
dislikes: Really nothing I can think of, every feature I have felt was missing since I started using it last year has been added since!
recommendations:
We previously used spreadsheets to track our finances (importing bank statements) which was becoming quite cumbersome as the company grew.

Xero has allowed us to stay on top of our finances through fast growth, given us more up to date and accurate figures to work with and allowed us to collaborate more effectively with our accountants. There are a whole bunch of time saving features including automated bank feeds, rules for automatically coding transactions, payroll features and invoice reminders.
use_case: I believe they still offer a free trial. It takes a fair bit of a time investment initially to give it the information it needs to help you but it's absolutely worth it!

likes: Xero is a much better platform for small business than QBO. Especially considering that most business owners are not an accountant.
dislikes: A few of their APIs have restrictions (especially around inventory)

bonus_data.title: Xero the best accounting for small business

likes: I like how easy it is to use and the pricing structure. I struggled a lot in quick books and it just seemed like the online version was too stripped down. Xero gave us the power back with an easy to use and robust cloud system.
dislikes: We have been awaiting payroll in our state since signing up, we are on to year 2 with Xero. Every time we ask it's either coming by then end of the year or we're told it's been pushed back.
recommendations: We needed a cloud based system that could be activated from anywhere. We also wanted the freedom to add as many users as needed the unlimited users was a huge selling point. Bonus was having an iOS app!
use_case: Don't join for the payroll. Templates are easy to use and adjust in word. Online payment integration is amazing!

switched_from.reason: Way easier to use, integrates really well with our additional cloud systems

likes: Xero has a clean, easy-to-understand interface that makes the learning curve short for new users. For accountants, it facilitates diagnosing and correcting client's mistakes. Xero has nice reporting which has recently been revised so that reports can be customized to fit a client's business. Reports can be downloaded in several formats which can be further manipulated in a spreadsheet application or sent as-is to the client.
dislikes: My dislikes are more along the lines of needed enhancements. Cash coding allows for bulk classification of transactions, but needs some work to streamline its interface with contacts. Bank feeds are facilitated by Yodlee which frequently requires refreshing to keep transactions up to date. Both are minor annoyances that are far outweighed by the efficiencies that Xero brings to the accountant. I would also love to see an interface with popular tax prep products such as Drake or ATX.
recommendations: Providing real time financial information to clients so they can make good business decisions. A big side benefit to my small business clients is the ability to target certain transaction timing to realize tax efficiencies and enhance tax planning capability.
use_case: Get an experienced professional to help set Xero up - although it can be done by a person with no experience with the software, it will most likely not be set up correctly and will require additional expertise to fix.

switched_from.reason: QuickBooks Online had significant stability issues, is difficult to use and difficult to diagnose and correct client errors.

likes: Xero has easy to use reporting tools to allow me to manage my annual, quarterly and monthly budgets. I can easily update my budget if circumstances change, record all of my outgoings and go back to review previous months and/ or compare with other time periods. It also allows me to group by expenditure type and stuff can electronically submit expense claims and allocate the expenditure type.
dislikes: When you are drilling down into the expenditure type it's difficult to get back to the overview report. you need to use the browser back button that usually it does not work at all and it is a bad experience for the user.
recommendations: Budget management
use_case: NA

likes:
I like the simple look-and-feel of the system.
Xero enables me to email invoices into the system and then enter the details another time.
The dashboard quickly shows me the money I have available and any invoices I need to chase up.
The VAT Return feature makes it very simple to calculate and submit my return - which could otherwise be quite time consuming.
dislikes:
I cannot think of anything that doesn't work how I would like it to in the main accounting system.
The payroll system doesn't seem to be able to automatically update with changes to staff's tax codes, which seems like quite a basic requirement.
recommendations: All businesses need accounts. This allows me to keep them up to date throughout the month.
use_case: Take advantage of the free trial, but make sure you know which features you'll need and which come with each plan. For example, during our trial we regularly put on foreign currency invoices, but then had to upgrade once the trial came to an end, because the basic plan doesn't include multi-currency.

Particularly for small businesses a great tool to do your own accounts and saving on having to hire too many accountants.
dislikes: Can't really think of that much negatives, even though I could imagine in larger organisations there will be certain features that could be improved. For a small business it's great as is!
recommendations: Doing our monthly accounts, expense claims, budget tracking etc.
use_case: My experience is as a small business owner, and my recommendation would be to try to do as much as you can with Xero yourself first. It is tempting to hire an accountant you will "run" Xero for you. However the main point of having Xero is that everyone (even if you don't have much financial background) can and should be doing their own accounts. You might still need an accountant for annual accounts or other things, but you'll be able to do a lot yourself!

reviewer_name: Verified User in Information Technology and Services

likes:
Xero is intuitive, I don't think I've even once had to look at the help files! In addition it prompts you in ways that are genuinely helpful, e.g. asking if you would like to email your invoices, or if you want to allow customers to view their bills on line. All real world, practical stuff.

The UI is well thought out too. The Dashboard can be tailored to individual preferences, but the default view shows logical high level views.

Xero also supports full API integration so you can wire it up with your CRM.
dislikes: Some of the Dashboard elements would be better if you could combine or collapse them (e.g. Bank Accounts - you might just want the overall cash position, not a list of all your accounts). But that aside this is minor and the platform is a great tool.
recommendations: Putting in new accounting package for start-up businesses.
use_case: Simple, intuitive, cloud based accounting software. Full feature set, can be tailored and integrated as required. Excellent package, highly recommended for any small to medium sized business.

At each stage you think you are done until you try to process payroll. The time it takes and the processes related to setting up electronic payments are unacceptable. 

The software is not set up to be compatible with some common web browsers

Xero says they will respond within 24 hours to questions but there is no assistance on weekends - when do they think small businesses do their accounting? 

Overall I still like the package but believe they are deleting negative reviews. The reviews I've read imply the software is outstanding, which it is not.

dislikes: Unfortunately, after using Xero for two months the bank feed stopped working. After going back and forth with Zero support for about a month they finally called me and told me that my problem was a known one that affects a handful of Xero users and at this time they have no workable solution for my account except recommend I manually import my bank transactions indefinitely, because they pretty much had no solution to my problem. I was told it could take days, or months or even never to resolve the problem. Had I known this would happen I would have gone with Quickbooks. When I asked if I could receive a discount on my service since it was handicapped they offered 30 days free for the month it took them to tell me they couldn't fix my problem but otherwise could not offer any further discounts.
recommendations: None, I hate the software.

The fact there is a known bug with no knowable time-line for fixing it is deplorable. Even worse is asking using users to forego the most important feature of Xero - it's user friendliness. I cannot in good faith recommend this program to anyone.

likes:
That it is fully web based and you can give access to anyone who may need to view and access your accounts.

It is also handy that you can control what functions different people have access too, so that someone in sales could not do journal entries for example.

It is also great that you can integrate it with your CRM as long as there is an interface available - albeit at an additional cost.

Being able to maintain all the bills and invoices online is perfect and reduces the paperwork in the office.
It is especially good if your clients or suppliers are also using Xero as it can transfer the invoices internally to the system which removes the need to rekey them - thus reducing any errors.

The rules that you can set up in the system to automatically match and reconcile bank payments is very useful, as is the ability to directly import your bank statements from many different banks.
dislikes: It is not necessarily intuitive, and you will need training from someone who knows what they are doing and I highly recommend working with an accountant who is familiar with it so that they can set it up and test it for you.
recommendations: Using Xero helps me to keep in touch and up to date with my accountants, so that we can easily share real time data to make business decisions.
use_case: Do take a look at Sage Online and other accounting packages to make sure this meets your need - but I would talk to your accountant as see what works for them, as they will most probably be the place you are getting support from.

There's also a few things that are really confusing (like attaching a file to the invoice, you need to attach it a couple of times to make it work) but according to Xero they're working on it.

Even them have problems with Xero and ask us (as account managers) to contact support. To be honest reviewer - this is a review of the user who is leaving Xero after 3 happy years, because the last year was quite unhappy and Xero just couldn't handle it.
As a side note I'd like to mention that from Xero support it slipped out that Xero company is not using Xero themselves! (as accounting software)
use_case: If you're a sole trader or 1 man band - Xero is for you. If you're planning to grow, avoid Xero as you'll have big costs involved in the future with moving to another accounting software.

yes fun because it's simple and laid out with the user in mind. So you don't need a degree in accounting just common sense. But don't take my word for it, just try it out, but i am sure that within one hour you see the value.

likes:
So, it's probably a bit of a stretch to say it's beautiful but it is extremely clean and easy to use. The interface is simple and intuitive, making using Xero extremely easy. Not only that, but the bank feeds make reconciling bank accounts a breeze.

It's well equipped too, coming with the usual array of accounting features such as balance sheet & profit and loss, and also management reports & budgeting. You can also use tracking and extra invoicing details to help create a wealth of important information you can use to run your business.

Finally, the clincher for me is the wealth of products that integrate in with Xero which provide a huge array of linked options for running your business, without re-keying data, which is a key time save for any small business.
dislikes: As highlighted by another use, journals aren't particularly well provided for. In addition, wages in the UK can't be used to submit anything to HMRC as they require & finally the integrations are often a one way affair which means that they aren't perfect, Xero needs to work more closely at working back with other providers.
recommendations: As an accountant, we need to be able to recommend simple & effective software to our clients, and Xero is perfect for this.
use_case: Take the time to trial the product, consider what you need for your business and also look at other products that may integrate in with Xero too, and how these will work together, and what that means for your date.

likes: I like Xero's cloud base accounting software - that I can log-in from anywhere.
dislikes: Lack of inventory/job costing interactivity.
recommendations: We are putting small businesses on Xero, especially those with younger owners who appreciate the cloud based nature.
use_case: Go through the certification process to become comfortable with Xero.

likes:
I prefer cloud-based accounting software like Xero because it allows me to interact easily with clients who aren't local. Xero is popular with my international clients and I was introduced to it through them. It was easy to learn and it's a nice looking package.

Creating identifying rules was incredibly easy and Xero does a great job of recognizing transactions.
dislikes:
Xero focuses on automation. It works best if you link it directly with bank accounts, credit cards, etc. But many clients are wary of that kind of access and prefer to provide me with Excel spreadsheets. Xero requires multiple steps in order to convert an Excel spreadsheet into data that Xero can understand and import. I think that integration with Excel is a pretty basic requirement for accounting software and Xero hasn't provided it.

Further more, manual entries are cumbersome. Xero forces you to do them one at a time, instead of providing a general ledger type of entry.

I realize that these complaints are a little "old school" and Xero is focused more on moving away from out of date bookkeeping methods - but not all of my clients are ready for new technology. As a result, I tend not to recommend Xero to anyone unless they are ready for complete automation.

Xero doesn't seem to make it easy for clients to grant direct access to their accountants. Most of my clients provide me with their actual login credentials. Actually, it does seem to be easy, just not obvious - Xero should work harder to make clients AND accountants aware of this feature. Xero requires that accountants endorse/sell Xero on their website in order to get free access. Quickbooks Online doesn't require this, and I think it discourages accountants from trying Xero.
recommendations: Becoming knowledgeable about Xero has opened my firm up to an international client base. Xero continues to be unpopular with my US clients (who prefer Quickbooks), but is by far the preferred software among my clients outside of the United States.
use_case: Xero is an accounting software that focuses on efficiency. It is not the software for people who like the "old ways" of manual entry or Excel spreadsheets. Xero is easiest to use if you're willing to give it direct access to bank accounts and credit cards.

bonus_data.title: Xero is fine, but needs work

likes:
Easy to use, easy to start, the company is international and growing, and the user fee is extremely cost effective and competitive! We have been on Xero Accounting for 5+ years (before anyone in the USA even knew about them)...
and I use it for my four businesses, and we are excited to convert to their payroll program shortly here in Colorado, which is also included with their base pricing. Once that is completed - what we will save on automated payroll processing (through intuit) more that pays for all four business licenses! I'm VERY IMPRESSED with both the product, up-time, simplicity of use, the continued upgrading of the product, as well as the company. I'm now trying to figure out how to invest in these guys from NZ, since their company growth rate exceeds 75% and they now have over 1/2 million small business users worldwide.
dislikes: Nothing - everyone in the company is extremely responsive, there is xero (pun intended) down time, and the bank feeds are an amazing time saver on reconciliation.
recommendations: Save time - which is the most valuable resource in a consulting business.
use_case: Do yourself a favor and make the switch - Xero is one business decision that was good 5+ years ago, and now is a "no-brainer" for anyone who does the research... the more you look and compare, the better they will look!

bonus_data.title: Amazing (simple), robust, cloud based accounting software

switched_from.reason:
Wanted to save time and use truly cloud based software that was developed for the cloud, from the ground up - not some "converted" code from the old days.

Blows anything I've seen in the marketplace for cost effectiveness, rich features and company responsiveness. It flat "works", while continually improving and enhancing - which is what you want any great software to do.

likes: Xero is incredibly easy to use. I really like the approving of transactions function.
dislikes: The overall format is a little annoying.

switched_from.reason: Xero is so so so much better.

likes: We moved off of FreeAgent on to Xero at the first of this year - and overall, it's pretty good. It ties up with ReceiptBank and is easier to do journals and manage cashflow for our investments and whatnot. FA started off for single agents and then moved to multiuser as their needs expanded. The customer support is pretty good.
dislikes:
It describes itself as multicurrency, but it's not, and it's clear that the design of the thing is just flawed.
There's a feature request from 2012 about making personal expenses work if you have expenses in another currency. You know, one of the basic multicurrency functions. 
It doesn't work, and the company line seems to be that it's a complicated feature. From a developer's point of view you'd expect that there'd be a module that would say something like "input money", and that they should be able to point the expenses function there, but no. Apparently it's too complicated.
And this is a product they market in the EU.

The whole multicurrency thing kind of doesn't work terribly well. It's as though someone added it without thinking about what it would be like for the user.
recommendations:
We:
- Run basic bookkeeping and VAT
- manage investments (loans, equity, and convertibles)
Need to manage cashflow
use_case: Need multicurrency? Don't use this.

Xero has helped us create a virtual accounting platform to manage the back end of the business and create a management dashboard leadership teams find helpful. Xero is a solid cloud accounting solution with some strong
use_case: Xero is a great solution for small businesses that need the basics from the accounting solution. It is a great, mobile solution. However, ensure you have a solution to get the right data that is clean and accurate into Xero from the beginning.

switched_from.reason: Xero is not a robust as QBO, but it is a lot easier to use. Additionally, the API seems to work better so you can customize the accounting solution with other software to address industry nuances.

likes: The user experience is strong, as is the ability to push invoices and purchases as drafts prior to running a review process. The tracking categories allow is to segment our financial data above and beyond the usual charts of accounts, which lets us track profitability by division very effectively.
dislikes: There are limits around API access, which can be frustrating. Also, the limit of two tracking categories is often too restrictive for the elements we'd like to track. The reporting is also somewhat limited.
recommendations: Clear understanding of business profitability and cashflow, simpler commission calculation, clear understanding of business performance per division. Strong product for
use_case: Good product, but a bit slow to evolve at the moment.

switched_from.reason: Saasu tracking categories were too loose and reporting and API were weaker than Xero.

likes:
The interface is easy to use and intuitive.
Reporting is average in the base, but can definitely be improved through leveraging one of the marketplace products.
The marketplace which has many options to meet vertical and general business needs is strong. There are many add-on solutions available with more being added as Xero continues to grow in popularity.
As an accountant I definitely prefer Xero over QBO at this point.
dislikes:
As has been mentioned by other users, the journal updating process can be a bit tedious. However, my experience has been that Xero has been willing to listen to their users and I suspect this will be addressed.
The reporting leaves room for improvement, but like I stated above, there are numerous tools available in the marketplace that offer great options.
recommendations:
We are using Xero internally to manage many of our accounting client's needs. After going through trials of both QuickBooks Online I feel that Xero provides for a smoother and 'friendlier' process for us and our clients.
Integrating with other business critical applications has been awesome! The amount of time savings alone through integrations with payroll and POS systems has been substantial.
use_case:
As an accounting process outsourcer, I definitely prefer Xero over the QuickBooks Online product. While QuickBooks Online has been working hard to catch up, I feel that Xero is still way ahead in the race simply due to them hitting the cloud accounting market first with a solid product.
If you find areas within Xero that you don't feel are adequately addressed from the application, check out the marketplace as there are some great solutions there. Furthermore, if you use another application that doesn't already have an integration with Xero, check out Zapier as an option to provide an integration.

likes: Ease of use. For basic functions there is no need to go through a long set up process.
dislikes: Some functionality beyond the basic is a little convoluted to get to, and whilst some reports are set up in a handy style for the technicalities of accounting, they are slightly too complex and technical for a causal user who just wants to see a bit of BI.
recommendations: Accounting for a small business. saves a lot of time and confusion over a manual process.
use_case: Get your account access to your profile so that his job (and your bill) is substantially reduced.

bonus_data.title: Good lightweight accounting tool for small businesses

likes: Simple and easy to understand. Run reports quickly. Bank recs are great.
dislikes: Very simple functionality, but that's expected. Will need to upgrade to an ERP.
recommendations: Accounting
use_case: If you're a small business go for it. Better than QB.

likes: I switched from Quickbooks because Xero is online. Since then I've grown to love many other things compared to Quickbooks. Bank feeds make data entry minimal and bank recs easy. Xero is rapidly growing and improving. Some features were limited a few years ago when I first starting using it, but the software is steadily improving. It gives me confidence to keep using it even if there are some short-term drawbacks. It runs extremely fast and is easy to use. The functions are intuitive and don't require extensive training to figure out. I like the integrations with other software. With some companies we use Unleashed Software for inventory and order management, and the integration with Xero works well.
dislikes: The reporting could be improved, which I know they are working on. Ability to customize is limited. The cash flow statement is terrible. For one company we go through a lot of extra work to prepare financials just because we need to prepare a better cash flow statement in Excel. The Global version doesn't have check printing capability, which is a large drawback. You can't batch pay invoices that aren't in the functional currency. It isn't a huge deal, but it takes extra work to pay individual bills and to reconcile separate payments in Xero with one payment in the bank account.
use_case: It is great for small companies because it is cost-effective and easy to use. It's core function is limited to basic accounting - bank accounts, AP, AR, Fixed Assets, and reporting. It does integrate with other specialized software for inventory and order management, CRM, etc, but large companies might prefer all-in-one software like Netsuite or SAP. So much better than

likes:
Firstly, I am a small, Australian business owner (turnover less than $1m) and I previously used MYOB. Unfortunately MYOB is not overly user friendly and therefore I employed a Bookkeeper, primarily to do our quarterly BAS. This obviously lead to increased costs. Since using Xero and gaining a small amount of training, I am not completely all invoicing, receiving and BAS accounting requirements myself. In addition to this, the way in which the auto-bank feeds work and matching of transactions is a breeze.
Reports in Xero also make life a lot easier. They allow my accountant to very quickly produce require reports including Profit & Loss etc. Although this is available through MYOB, it is not as easy to use.
Also in our business we exclusive use Apple products and I have found no integration issues with Safari or other browsers.
Xero also has an iPhone App however it's current features are quite limited. I don't tend to use to much currently however may look at it again next Quarter.
dislikes: I honestly haven't yet found an issue. That said, I don't currently use the Payroll component nor need to worry about currency conversion.
recommendations: Saving of time and money is a huge benefit. I was also paying $58/month with MYOB but only $50/month with Xero. As opposed to employing someone to manage the accounting of my business, I can do some of it myself. It is important to note that during setup and for some parts of the business account, an accounting professional is required. I don't believe this is a product that will allow you to completely disengaged with an accountant (and nor should it).
use_case: Have a crack at the free trail period first to decide if Xero is for you.

bonus_data.title: Xero - hell of a lot better than MYOB

likes: The automatic imports of bank & credit card feeds keep all of our transactions in Xero automatically. The rules for similar transactions are easy to set up and make maintaining our books as easy as the click of a button. All of the built-in reports help us always stay up to date on cash flow and balances.
dislikes: Nothing comes to mind here... it's honestly incredibly easy to use and way more flexible than the alternatives on the market.
recommendations: We've been able to keep all of our bookkeeping in-house and rarely have to import/upload transactions, plus everything's always up to date with the automatic feeds and rules. Xero saves me several hours every month and eliminates the thousands of dollars we'd be spending on a bookkeeper.
use_case: The cost savings alone is tremendous here - the difference between $40/month and a bookkeeper expense is really significant to our business. Additionally, being able to manage the books as the owner makes sure nothing is lost in translation with the bookkeeper

bonus_data.title: Xero keeps our bookkeeping easy and gives us constant insight with great reporting

likes: Easy to use and intuitive. I've found Xero much easier to work with than QuickBooks, and the support team are amazingly responsive.
dislikes: UK payroll still a little under baked. Feels like they rushed it through a bit.
use_case: Managing business accounts and compliance.

bonus_data.title: Great product, and even greater support

likes: I am reviewing all the comments and so far I find them very helpful.
dislikes: I am disappointed to find they are not handling multiple currencies very well. For me that is a vital feature for my business.

reviewer_name: Verified User in Import and Export

bonus_data.title: Looking for online accounting option other than QuickBooks

likes: Mobility is the Number one reason why I moved over. I was a QuickBooks user for years but having to run an application on a local PC just became too inconvenient. I love being able to anything from anywhere on any device!
dislikes: The Dislike is a bit more difficult to nail down. In the beginning of the product it was the fact the Payroll was not included. Now they have that so it is a full package.
recommendations: The fact that I can create invoices or record expenses anywhere at any time solves my biggest Problem. Setting time aside to do it. This had made me much more efficient and completely revised my business processes. From
use_case: I have found this to be the most feature rich and cost effective product around.

likes: I am an accounting professional working in industry so we had the need for a cloud based system that was cost-effective. Xero has a clean interface and is very easy to navigate around, make transactions, amend transactions, and expenses. We can all (three users in total) log in to Xero at the same time and this is an advantage over other Accounting products. The exporting of data into excel via the GL export feature is very handy as this allows for management accounts to be created directly in excel. Xero also allows for for than one tracking (department) meaning you can have department, location, and cost centres all recorded on the one invoice.
dislikes: There is a fairly manual process of updating journals, which I use regularly so this is one area they could improve. However it would be safe to assume the majority of Xero end users would not need to use journals. This is because journals are normally done by accounting professionals rather than small business owners.
recommendations: Xero allows for uploading of files with each transactions meaning email confirmations and order forms can be at your disposal with one click. Bank reconcilliations are simpler because many invoices are already matched based on amount therefore the time has been halved. We have also been able to quickly generate reports in Xero. For example, an aged receivables report would normally be done on its own where-as Xero allows you to create a detailed report (invoice line by line). This is useful for credit control activities.
use_case: Take time to upload all transactions with the same tracking/Chart of accounts you intend to use for the foreseeable future.

likes:
There are a lot of things I love about Xero. From the ability to do everything from my phone, to the way it integrates so easily with our POS, to how it'll work once we switch our payroll over to a better provider. The few times I've needed to get support from them they've been fantastic, and the community is great. Each section also has a "Help" link that goes directly to the online help for that section. I can't even begin to count how much time Xero has saved me.

I genuinely wish I had more time just so I could spend more time working in Xero.
dislikes: Wish our "small town bank" played nicer.
recommendations: I wear many hats in our business. From CFO to HR to Customer Service to Baker to Buttercream Maker to Gopher. Add in the Husband and Dad hats and you're left with little to no time. With Xero by the time I get home I don't have to worry about the accounting. I'm able to do it from my phone while I'm in line getting a coffee, or waiting for an oven timer to go off. Amazing
use_case: Check the list of addons for Xero and seriously also consider switching some services over. We used Square before Xero integrated and now I couldn't imagine life without it. Switching our payroll over to a provider that integrates as well. When you have 20 hour days saving even just minutes can make a huge difference.

likes:
Business software is the backbone of any company or organization. Deciding on the best solution can be nerve-wracking, as you can end up staking your reputation and the health of the business on your decision. Not making the right decision can result in dire consequences. Unfortunately some of you reading this know exactly what I mean. You want to make sure you've allied yourself with the smartest, best software designers from the very beginning that ultimately help make things easier instead of harder. Fortunately, you can find that solution by signing up with Xero. 

Xero has been often referred to as the 'Apple of accounting', which is honestly fair. And if you're not an Apple person, that's okay because it's not platform specific. It works on your PC, your Mac, your Ubuntu laptop, your Android tablet, and your oversized iPhone - it's integrated. 

What you get when you decide to utilize Xero is a collaborative tool, brilliantly designed with unlimited users for about $30-$70/month, depending on your needs. In terms of pricing there isn't an outrageous flat fee that is typically bundled with ERP solutions. So, that means that you pay as you go and as long as you're happy with the system. 

It's unique in terms of the level of customization. They have a marketplace for something called 'Add Ons', which are intended to help you tailor the system to your specific needs. It's very similar to an App Store with Add Ons being like Apps tailored to your business. Run a T-shirt company? There's an Add On for that. Run a plumbing business? There's an Add On for that too. 

Perhaps the best aspect of the software is something very simple - it's on the cloud. Sometimes this can concern business leaders as it means you're using the big, scary internet to handle your accounting. But, it's worth keeping in mind that your bank is on the cloud too. And Xero's security measures are the same if not better, and their track record shows this. 

The real benefit of having your system on the cloud, however, is that your entire team is exactly on the same page. Xero started as a company with this in mind from the outset. In the past as an Accountant, I used to be constantly asked if I could create an invoice or whether it had been paid. With Xero I don't really get asked these questions because our team has access to the system and can look the items up themselves. And it helps give departments agency in terms of billing, because it means that they don't have to completely rely on the Accounting Department for their work. 

Also, working on the cloud means that there isn't a file to send your CPA. You end up looking at the same financials and are able to collaborate real time with your business advisors and partners. 

In short, Xero made my life easier and became like a valued advisor. It guides you in terms of getting your company up and running, and can go toe to toe with most ERP's. And, most importantly, it's able to accommodate a quickly growing company.

Steve Jobs once said that most accounting software is designed to leave people confused - if finance is complicated, certainly the software should be complicated too. The complete opposite is the case here - clear, understandable, insightful, useful. Highly recommended.
dislikes:
P.O. functionality and reporting could be a bit better, but they're working on it. If you aren't familiar with pivot tables, it can be a bit more effort to get the information you need. However, if you are familiar with pivot tables the world is your oyster. 

It's not necessarily for everyone. Some people can't think differently from Quickbooks. And that's okay. But, those who are more tech-oriented and grew up with all things internet will feel more at home here.
recommendations:
If you've ever reconciled a bank account before you know what a cumbersome horrible process it usually is. Where Xero really excels is in terms of design. Any designer's first itch is to take the worst part of a problem and really tackle it. 

What they've done with the reconciliation process is truly incredible. Xero predicts your transactions and lines them up for you. What results is what's been called the gameification of business software. 

Instead of it being a chore it becomes almost alarmingly fun. And it results in knowing exactly where you are in terms of your financial status. 

Most other accounting solutions present the idea of a dashboard, but don't deliver. You see pie charts and graphs, but they're not really useful. It's like a false promise - this looks really cool and we know that it should work this way, but we don't know really how to do that. With Xero they excel at it and the dashboard becomes completely interactive so that you're able to drill down and see the exact transaction that's at the heart of an issue. Even better, you're able to edit it on the spot. 

So, with good design comes the awareness of what's going on. And in terms of accounting I really believe that's the whole point. You're supposed to know where things are and report on where they went. Xero empowers that. Clear Information,
use_case:
Try the demo for free first and see if the system works for you. 

Compare it to how other systems are being pitched to you and test whether you are able to do the basics and can see yourself operating in it day to day. Consider getting a new chart of accounts as this is your opportunity to clean house. Collaborate with your CPA or financial advisor and don't hesitate to contact Xero for additional help. Their customer service is top notch.

User friendly
Brilliant support service

Allocations of credits is cumbersome
Audit trail is not as good as other packages

likes:
The Xero online accounting product is best fitted for progressive technology driven small businesses that want to control the design of their accounting systems and integrate them with other small business functions (inventory, CRM, HR). Xero has excellent customer service when migrating or setting up a new company. They worked with the bank and my small business clients to ensure bank feeds were setup properly. Xero's add-on partners support the Xero product with features that may be lacking.

Xero's companion mobile app for Android and iOS provides mobile small businesses with access on the go to actually perform business functions (account reconciliations) instead of just a snapshot of account balances.
dislikes:
Currently Xero does not offer payroll for all U.S. states and the financial reporting functions leave a bit to be desired. A quick search of the add-on partners may provide some small businesses with additional reporting capabilities. 

Still waiting on U.S. tax software integration also.
recommendations: Small business owners who have multiple users of their accounting software in addition to outsourced consultants (bookkeepers, accountants, and other financial consultants) who need access to financial data will be able to simultaneously use the Xero product. This alleviates the need to send data files back and forth which typically can cause data corruption. Xero's reconciliation functions also make account reconciling smoother and less time consuming. Almost Zero
use_case: U.S. Small business owners looking to Xero for their accounting needs want to be sure that their state payroll is included (if not be sure to have one that integrates with Xero), their reporting functions will be addressed, and check the add-on partners for additional integration.

likes:
The navigation from screen to screen is very intuitive. Being web based you are able to open multiple screens.

This online accounting software is a full accounting solution with traditional features such as chart of accounts, aged accounts receivable reports and general ledgers. It can also manage more complex accounting tasks such as fixed assets and depreciation
dislikes:
The VAT return is quite inflexible in terms of what you can include and exclude from the return. There needs to be more options around making manual adjustments to the return. It would also make the product more useable if it could handle partial exemption companies.

An additional option to produce statutory accounts in IXBRL format would also be very useful.

The link to the bank account does not always work well. There are often transactions that are missed off. Fixing this bug would make the software much better.
recommendations:
It is used to manage suppliers and customers. It is the basis for producing management accounts and cost center reports. 

The dashboard is useful for giving a snap shot on a daily basis. It is helping with cash management and tracking suppliers. 

The P&L report summary is very useful at the end of the month for getting the management accounts right. It would be useful to be able to see a fuller summary on screen as sometimes the report is very wide.
use_case: Need to make the VAT return more flexible - so it can support partial exemption. It also needs to allow for manual adjustments to the return.

likes: If you've used other accounting system, Xero is pain relief. We've been using it since starting out as a two person "hey, this is an interesting idea" outfit (B2B space) - now at 30+ staff across multiple continents, multiple funding rounds, etc. We've considered switching our CRM systems (Salesforce), we've considered changing helpdesk (Zendesk), and others - but Xero has been the one system we are pretty happy with as is. Not that it couldn't improve though.
dislikes: SAAS model makes revenue recognition painful - we accept $ up front, but recognize it over 12 months. This is a fairly common model in SAAS, but is painful to configure in Xero - i.e. requires splitting invoices, etc. This needs fixing. Reporting is more painful than it needs to be - use Spotlight (Xero should just buy them). Expense reporting needs an offline app for iOS/Android.
recommendations: With Xero - financials, visibility, planning. We're happy with what it's allowing us to do. SAAS startup using
use_case: Buy Spotlight.

The interface is a little spartan and dated, and I wish you could do a tad more visual branding in the dashboard so the internal staff are working with my brand represented better, but it's workable.
recommendations: In this case, what would take expensive software and lots of staff training, I can now delegate to my bookkeeper offsite or onsite and the work gets done, with the reporting fed back to me in a format so that I can make good business decisions. Great Product, Great Small Business
use_case: This is a more-than-competent accounting and bookkeeping software for small business. Price, convenience of being Web-based, and great feature-set makes it a rock-solid choice for myself and my clients.

We are so happy that we switched over from Quickbooks and plan on only taking Xero clients moving forward.

I was able to reduce my overhead, take on additional clients, and save our existing clients money. I wish I would have switched to Xero when it first became available to the US Market. Xero- Head and Shoulders Above The
use_case: Don't even bother with outdated software- try Xero and you will wonder why you didn't switch earlier. I am a total Xero fanatic and you will probably become one too! :)

Its also great the way you can either link it to your bank where possible or simply upload bank statements in an excel file. It also makes tracking who you paid, when you paid and searching for past payments.
dislikes: Xero's biggest issue is that is doesn't yet automatically tie in with all banks so we currently have to upload our bank statements on to an excel and then upload them to xero, but once they are up there it's fine, you just need to make sure your not uploading any duplicate lines on your statement.
use_case: Be sure about how xero will tie in with your business and make sure you take advice from a xero professional finance rep near you, listen to what they tell you when they are training you and dont be afraid of trying it.

likes: Bank feed integrations and invoicing tools make it easy to use Xero on a daily basis.
dislikes: Sometimes it is difficult to navigate in Xero but after a few moment of jogging my memory I can usually find what I need.
use_case: We use Xero for accounts payable & receivables alongside all other elements of our day to day banking & accounting. It's a great platform as an all-in-one solution.

likes: Bank Feeds directly into Xero. Quick and easy reconciliation
dislikes: Lack of support for multi-currency expense reports. (Xero handles multi-currency invoices and other things, but not expense reports).
use_case: Xero is the first Accountancy tool where I didn't need a month to understand the terminology. Xero gives you a "sandbox" environment where you can test out new things if you are not sure, and an excellent help center. Cloud-based Small
